items.title: Police Trainee (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These regulations amend the Police Trainee Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 and the Police (Recruitment) (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2001.

items.title: The Cross-border Railway Services (Working Time) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations implement the provisions of Council Directive 2005/47/EC (OJ No. L195, 27.7.05, p. 15) of 18th July 2005 on the Agreement between the Community of European Railways (CER) and the European Transport Workers' Federation (ETF) on certain aspects of the working conditions of mobile workers engaged in interoperable cross-border services in the railway sector.

items.title: The Cycle Routes Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
Fisheries (Amendment) Byelaws (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Byelaws amend the Fisheries Byelaws (Northern Ireland) 2003.

items.title: Fisheries (Conservation of Coarse Fish) Byelaws (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Byelaws:—

items.title: The Coroners (Practice and Procedure) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Rules amend the Coroners (Practice and Procedure) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1963 so as to—

items.title: Milk and Milk Products (Pupils in Educational Establishments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Milk and Milk Products (Pupils in Educational Establishments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 (S.R. 2001 No. 129) and the Milk and Milk Products (Pupils in Educational Establishments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (S.R. 2007 No. 490).

items.title: The Further Education (Student Support) (Eligibility) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations provide for financial support towards tuition fees to institutions of further education for students who are domiciled in a part of the United Kingdom other than Northern Ireland, in the European Community, or the European Economic Area, but are studying at Colleges of Further Education in Northern Ireland, in respect of the academic year beginning on or after 1st September 2008 and ending on 31st August 2009.

items.title: Police (Testing for Substance Misuse) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These regulations make provision for drug and alcohol tests for police officers serving in the PSNI and PSNI Reserve, police trainees and police reserve trainees.

items.title: The Fixed-term Employees (Prevention of Less Favourable Treatment) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Fixed-term Employees (Prevention of Less Favourable Treatment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2002 (“the 2002 Regulations”).

items.title: The Westlink (Busways) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations provide for the regulation of lengths of special road described in the Schedule.

items.title: Foyle Area and Carlingford Area (Angling) (Amendment) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Foyle Area and Carlingford Area (Angling) Regulations 2001 by prohibiting angling on a stretch of the River Finn upstream of Cloghan Bridge, County Donegal.

items.title: The Child Maintenance (2008 Act) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Article 2 of this Order brings into operation provisions of the Child Maintenance Act (Northern Ireland) 2008. The provision in section 33 of the Act, which extinguishes liability in respect of fees and interest, is brought into operation on 5th August 2008.

items.title: The Public Health (Ships) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Public Health (Ships) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1971.

items.title: The Specified Animal Pathogens Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts with amendments the Specified Animal Pathogens Order (Northern Ireland) 1999 (S. R. 1999 No. 434).

items.title: The Welfare Reform (2007 Act) (Commencement No. 6 and Transitional and Savings Provisions) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Article 2 of this Order provides for the coming into operation of the following provisions of the Welfare Reform Act (Northern Ireland) 2007—

items.title: Less Favoured Area Compensatory Allowances Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations provide for the implementation of Articles 13(a), 14(1), 14(2) first and second indents, and 15 of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1257/1999 (O.J. No. L160, 26.06.1999, p. 80) as last amended by Council Regulation (EC) No. 1698/2005 (O.J. No. L277, 21.10.2005, p. 1). They also provide for the implementation of Measure 2.1 of the Northern Ireland Rural Development Programme approved by Commission Decision C(2007) 4411.

items.title: The Zoonoses (Monitoring) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations provide inspectors with powers of entry to monitor for zoonoses and antimicrobial resistance to zoonotic agents and other agents that pose a threat to public health, as required by Directive 2003/99/EC (on the monitoring of zoonoses and zoonotic agents, amending Council Decision 90/424/EEC and repealing Council Directive 92/117/EEC) (regulation 4).

items.title: The Road Traffic (Traffic Wardens) (Revocation) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order revokes the Road Traffic (Traffic Wardens) Order (Northern Ireland) 1999.

items.title: The Welfare Reform Act (Relevant Statutory Provision) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Section 39(1) of the Welfare Reform Act (Northern Ireland) 2007 (“the Act”) provides that information relating to certain benefits may be supplied to certain persons for purposes connected with the application of grant paid under a “relevant statutory provision” towards expenditure related to welfare services. This Order provides that a relevant statutory provision is Article 4 of the Housing Support Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2002. That Article provides that the Northern Ireland Housing Executive may pay grants to eligible persons towards expenditure incurred by them in relation to housing support services.

items.title: The Social Security (Use of Information for Housing Benefit and Welfare Services Purposes) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Section 39(2) of the Welfare Reform Act (Northern Ireland) 2007 (“the 2007 Act”) provides that certain persons (“relevant persons”) who hold information for a prescribed purpose may use that information for another prescribed purpose. Section 39(2) also allows such persons to provide that information to other relevant persons for use in relation to the same or another prescribed purpose. These Regulations prescribe those purposes.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Speed Limits) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Speed Limits) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1989 by inserting a definition for the Road Traffic Regulation (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 and by substituting a new regulation 2.

items.title: The Establishments and Agencies (Fitness of Workers) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations which apply to establishments and agencies in Northern Ireland, amend certain Regulations made under the Health and Personal Social Services (Quality, Improvement and Regulation) (Northern Ireland) Order 2003 in respect of nursing homes, residential care homes, nursing agencies, children’s homes, day care settings and domiciliary care agencies.

items.title: The Magistrates’ Courts (Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates’ Courts (Children (Northern Ireland) Order 1995) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1996 (S.R. 1996 No. 323) in consequence of the Children (Emergency Protection Orders) Act (Northern Ireland) 2007 (c.3) to:

items.title: The Health and Personal Social Services (Superannuation) (Additional Voluntary Contributions, Injury Benefits and Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Health and Personal Social Services (Superannuation) (Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (S.R. 1999 No. 294) (“the AVC Regulations”), the Health and Personal Social Services (Injury Benefits) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 (S.R. 2001 No. 367) (“the Injury Benefits Regulations”) and the Health and Personal Social Services (Compensation for Premature Retirement) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1983 (S.R. 1983 No. 155) (“the Compensation Regulations”).

items.title: The Mesothelioma, etc., (2008 Act) (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of provisions of the Mesothelioma, etc., Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 as follows—

items.title: The Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers’ Compensation) (Payment of Claims) (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers’ Compensation) (Payment of Claims) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988 to provide that certain payments under the Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers’ Compensation) (Northern Ireland) Order 1979 are reduced by the amount of any lump sum payment in respect of diffuse mesothelioma made under section 1 of the Mesothelioma, etc., Act (Northern Ireland) 2008.

items.title: The Mesothelioma Lump Sum Payments (Claims and Reconsiderations) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations provide for claims for a lump sum payment for diffuse mesothelioma under section 1 of the Mesothelioma, etc., Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (“the Act”) and for reconsideration of a determination on such a claim.

items.title: The Mesothelioma Lump Sum Payments (Conditions and Amounts) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations provide for a lump sum payment under section 1 of the Mesothelioma, etc., Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (“the Act”) to a person with diffuse mesothelioma or the person’s dependant.

items.title: The Social Security (Recovery of Benefits) (Lump Sum Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the recovery of lump sum payments to which Article 3A(2) of the Social Security (Recovery of Benefits) (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (“the Order”) applies.

items.title: The Statutory Sick Pay (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Statutory Sick Pay (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1982 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Fund (Applications and Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations consolidate and recast, with the amendments referred to below, the provisions of the Social Fund (Applications) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”) and the Social Fund (Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”).

items.title: The Zoonoses and Animal By-Products (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which apply in Northern Ireland, revoke the Breeding Flocks, Hatcheries and Animal By-Products (Fees) Order (Northern Ireland) 2004.

items.title: The Police Support Staff (Transfer of Employment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations transfer civil servants who are seconded to work as police support staff to the employment of the Northern Ireland Policing Board with effect from 1st October 2008.

items.title: The Magistrates’ Courts (Amendment No. 2) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates’ Courts Rules (Northern Ireland) 1984 (‘the principal Rules’) so as to amend the procedure in relation to the giving of notice of the intention to adduce or elicit evidence of bad character and hearsay evidence, and to take account of Part III of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2004 (‘the 2004 Order’), Article 21A of the Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1999 (‘the 1999 Order’).

items.title: The Magistrates’ Courts (Detention and Forfeiture of Seized Cash) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates’ Courts (Detention and Forfeiture of Seized Cash) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2003 [S.R. 2003 No.17] (‘the principal Rules’) to take account of the amendments made to Part 5 and Chapter 3 of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002, by the Serious Crime Act 2007 and which extend the category of investigators entitled to seize and detain cash to include accredited financial investigators.

items.title: The Magistrates’ Courts (Costs in Criminal Cases) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates’ Courts (Costs in Criminal Cases) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1988 so as provide that rule 5 refers to an order made under section 2 or 3 of the Costs in Criminal Cases Act (Northern Ireland) 1968.

items.title: The Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Personal and Occupational Pension Schemes (Protected Rights) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the Protected Rights Regulations”) and the Personal Pension Schemes (Appropriate Schemes) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the Appropriate Schemes Regulations”).

items.title: The Pensions (2008 Act) (Actuarial Guidance) (Consequential Provisions) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order amends certain occupational, personal and stakeholder pension scheme regulations in consequence of section 15 of, and Schedule 5 to, the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 which remove the requirement for the Department for Social Development to approve certain actuarial Guidance Notes and a Technical Memorandum.

items.title: The Zootechnical Standards (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Zootechnical Standards Regulations 1992, S.R. 1992 No. 438 (“the 1992 Regulations”).

items.title: The Occupational Pension Schemes (Transfer Values) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to provisions about the calculation and verification of transfer values (the amount that may be transferred from one pension scheme to another). They set out the new manner for calculating and verifying cash equivalents and cash transfer sums.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 and the Housing Benefit (Persons who have attained the qualifying age for state pension credit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006.

items.title: The Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) (Amendment No.2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: The Planning (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (“the 1999 Regulations”) implemented, for Northern Ireland, Council Directive 85/337/EEC (“the EIA Directive”) on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private projects on the environment (O.J. No. L175, 5.7.1985, p.40) as amended by Council Directive 97/11/EC (O.J. No. L73, 14.3.1997, p.5). The EIA Directive was also amended by Council Directive 2003/35/EC (O.J. No. L156, 25.6.2003, p.17).

items.title: The Producer Responsibility Obligations (Packaging Waste) (Amendment No.2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Producer Responsibility Obligations (Packaging Waste) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (the “2007 Regulations”). The 2007 Regulations impose on exporters an obligation to be accredited by the Department before they can issue a packaging waste export recovery note, as evidence of the export of the tonnage of packaging waste specified in the note for reprocessing outside the United Kingdom. These Regulations revise the requirements for accreditation of an exporter imposed by the 2007 Regulations.

items.title: The Maternity and Parental Leave etc. and the Paternity and Adoption Leave (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend provisions in two Statutory Rules relating to statutory maternity leave and adoption leave, namely the Maternity and Parental Leave etc. Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (S.R. 1999 No. 471) (“the 1999 Regulations”) and the Paternity and Adoption Leave Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2002 (S.R. 2002 No. 377) (“the 2002 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Incapacity) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (General Benefit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1984 (“the General Benefit Regulations”), the Social Security (Incapacity Benefit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994 (“the Incapacity Benefit Regulations”) and the Social Security (Incapacity for Work) (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the Incapacity for Work Regulations”).

items.title: Grammar Schools (Charges) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Grammar Schools (Charges) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 and revoke the Grammar Schools (Charges) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006.

items.title: Provision of Health Services to Persons not Ordinarily Resident (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These regulations amend the Provision of Health Services to Persons not Ordinarily Resident Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 (“the principal Regulations”), which make provision as to the entitlement of, and exemptions from charges for services forming part of health services which may be provided to visitors to Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Employment and Support Allowance Consequential Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend various sets of Regulations relating to housing benefit in connection with the introduction of employment and support allowance from 27th October 2008.

items.title: The Companies (1986 Order) (Annual Return) and Companies (Principal Business Activities) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations repeal, with effect from 1 October 2008, certain provisions in Article 372 of the Companies (Northern Ireland) Order 1986 (S.I. 1986/1032 (N.I. 6)) (“the 1986 Order”), which specifies information to be included in the annual return delivered to the registrar of companies under Article 371 of the 1986 Order. These provisions include the requirement for companies to list the other directorships and former names of individual directors; the other directorships of corporate directors and the requirement for private companies to state whether resolutions are in operation enabling the company to dispense with the laying of accounts and reports before the company in general meeting, or to dispense with the holding of annual general meetings.

items.title: The Bluetongue Order (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order continues to implement Council Directive 2000/75/EC laying down specific provisions for the control and eradication of bluetongue. It revokes and remakes the Bluetongue Order (Northern Ireland) 2003 in order to enforce Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1266/2007.

items.title: Rural Development (Financial Assistance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 15th October 2008, supplement the Community legislation listed in the Schedule to the Regulations (“the Community legislation”). The Community legislation inter alia provides for assistance to be paid from the European Agricultural Fund for Rural Development (EAFRD) towards operations which promote rural development by facilitating the improvement of the quality of life in rural areas and diversification of the rural economy. They also provide for the use of local action groups to assist with the delivery of rural development. The Northern Ireland Rural Development Programme 2007-2013 is co-funded by the EAFRD and the UK Exchequer.

items.title: The New Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order amends The New Firefighters’ Pension Scheme Order (Northern Ireland) 2007. Some of the amendments correct errors in the New Firefighters’ Pension Scheme. Others introduce new provisions.

items.title: The Firefighters’ Pension Scheme (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This order amends the Firefighters’ Pension Scheme Order (Northern Ireland) 2007. The amendment that introduces new article 16A entitlement to two pensions (made by article 2 and paragraph 1 of the Schedule) has effect from 1st April 2007. The amendment made by article 2 and paragraph 3 of the Schedule has effect from the 1st of April 2006. The other amendments have effect from 1st July 2007. The power to give retrospective effect is conferred by article 10 of the Fire Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1984, as continued by Article 60(1) and (2) of the Fire and Rescue Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006.

items.title: Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional Provisions) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 6th October 2008 provisions of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 dealing with guidance to agencies on assessing and managing certain risks to the public and changes to the enforcement of certain court orders made on conviction.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Taxi Drivers’ Licences) (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 4(1) of the Motor Vehicles (Taxi Drivers’ Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1991 by increasing the fee for a first licence from £110.00 to £140.00 and the fee for the renewal of a licence from £75.00 to £105.00.

items.title: The Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes (Transfer Values) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Personal Pension Schemes (Transfer Values) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Occupational Pension Schemes (Transfer Values) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 and the Occupational Pension Schemes (Early Leavers: Cash Transfer Sums and Contribution Refunds) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006. Those Regulations set out the manner of calculating and verifying transfer values (the amount which may be transferred from one pension scheme to another).

items.title: Agriculture (Student fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Agriculture (Student Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007, so as to substitute a new level of fee to be paid by persons undertaking a course of higher education provided by the Department of Agriculture and Rural Development (Regulations 4(1) and 5(2)). The Regulations also further clarify provision for the Department to apply fees to courses of further education (Regulation 4(2)), and correct an error (Regulation 1(1) and 4(1)). These Regulations revoke the Agriculture (Student fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007.

items.title: The Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2004 (“the principal Regulations”), which provide for remission and payment of certain charges which would otherwise be payable under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 and for the payment by the Department of Health, Social Services and Public Safety of travelling expenses incurred in attending a hospital.

items.title: The Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges (Amendment No. 3) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2004 (“the principal Regulations”), which provide for remission and payment of certain charges which would otherwise be payable under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 and for payment by the Department of Health, Social Services and Public safety of travelling expenses incurred in attending a hospital.

items.title: The European Fisheries Fund (Grants) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations provide that the Department may make payments for the purposes of Title IV of Council Regulation (EC) 1198/2006 on the European Fisheries Fund.

items.title: General Dental Services (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Health and Personal Social Services General Dental Services Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993 (“the principal Regulations”) which regulate the terms on which general dental services are provided under the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972.

items.title: The Libraries (2008 Act) (Commencement No 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of Sections 1 (part), 3, 4,5 9 and 10 (1) and Schedules 1 and 3 of the Libraries Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 relating to establishing the body to be known as the Northern Ireland Library Authority and giving it specified powers.

items.title: The Salaries (Assembly Ombudsman and Commissioner for Complaints) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order provides for an increase in the annual salaries payable to the Assembly Ombudsman for Northern Ireland and the Northern Ireland Commissioner for Complaints.

items.title: The Child Maintenance (2008 Act) (Commencement No. 3 and Transitional and Savings Provisions) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order brings into operation provisions of the Child Maintenance Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (c. 10 (N.I.)) (“the Act”) as follows:

items.title: The Street Works (Inspection Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend The Street Works (Inspection Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 by increasing the fee prescribed for each chargeable inspection from £24.00 to £25.00.

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) (Amendment No.2) 2008
items.description: These Rules amend the Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) 1980 (SR 1980/346) so as to—

items.title: The Child Support, Pensions and Social Security (2000 Act) (Commencement No. 11) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of provisions of the Child Support, Pensions and Social Security Act (Northern Ireland) 2000, in so far as not already in operation, as follows—

items.title: The Child Support Information Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations provide for the furnishing of information or evidence required by the Department for Social Development for the purpose of its functions under the Child Support (Northern Ireland) Order 1991 (“the Order”). They also provide for the disclosure by the Department of information held for the purpose of the Order. These Regulations replace the Child Support (Information, Evidence and Disclosure) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (“the 1992 Regulations”).

items.title: The Child Support (Consequential Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations make provision consequential on the coming into operation of section 7 of the Child Maintenance Act (Northern Ireland) 2008, which repeals Articles 9 and 43 of the Child Support (Northern Ireland) Order 1991.

items.title: The Infant Formula and Follow-on Formula (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Infant Formula and Follow-on Formula Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (S.R. 2007 No. 506) (“the 2007 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Child Maintenance Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Income Support Regulations”), the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations”) and the Social Security (Working Tax Credit and Child Tax Credit Consequential Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003.

items.title: Game Preservation (Special Protection for Irish Hares) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order prohibits a person from killing, taking, selling or purchasing Irish hares during the period outlined in Article 2.

items.title: The Police and Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1989 (Codes of Practice) (No. 4) Order 2008
items.description: Article 2 of this Order appoints 10th November 2008 as the date on which a new code of practice pursuant to Article 65 of the Police and Criminal Evidence (Northern Ireland) Order 1989 will come into operation. The new code of practice (Code H) makes specific provision for the detention, treatment and questioning by police officers of persons arrested under section 41 of the Terrorism Act 2000.

items.title: The Child Support and Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: The powers exercised to make these Regulations are those contained in the Child Support (Northern Ireland) Order 1991 (“the Order”). Some of those powers are conferred by provisions of the Order prior to the amendments made to it by the Child Support, Pensions and Social Security Act (Northern Ireland) 2000 (“the 2000 Act”), some of which amendments are not fully in operation, and relate to the child support scheme which was in operation prior to 3rd March 2003, and which remains in operation for the purposes of certain cases (“the old scheme”). Other powers are conferred by provisions of the Order as amended by the 2000 Act, which relate to the child support scheme provided for by those amendments, which came into operation for the purposes of specified categories of cases on 3rd March 2003 (see the Child Support, Pensions and Social Security (2000 Act) (Commencement No. 9) Order (Northern Ireland) 2003) (“the new scheme”). These Regulations also exercise powers inserted into the Order by the Child Maintenance Act (Northern Ireland) 2008.

items.title: The Miscellaneous Food Additives (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Miscellaneous Food Additives Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (S.R. 1996 No.50 as amended) (“the Additives Regulations”) in order to rectify an error caused by a defect in the Miscellaneous Additives and the Sweeteners in Food (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (S.R. 2007 No.325).

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 3) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the State Pension Credit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003, the Housing Benefit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 and the Housing Benefit (Persons who have attained the qualifying age for state pension credit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006.

items.title: The Social Fund (Cold Weather Payments) (General) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These regulations amend the Social Fund (Cold Weather Payments) (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1988 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Employment and Support Allowance (Consequential Provisions No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations make provisions consequential upon the introduction of employment and support allowance by inserting references to employment and support allowance where appropriate in—

items.title: The Employment and Support Allowance (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations make various amendments in connection with the introduction of employment and support allowance from 27th October 2008.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations consolidate the Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 (S.R. 2003 No.450) and the Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (S.R. 2007 No.17), which declared fuels to be authorised fuels for the purposes of Article 2(2) of the Clean Air (Northern Ireland) Order 1981. They also revoke those Regulations. Nine additional fuels are authorised (Schedule, paragraphs 4, 9, 18, 27, 32, 34, 43, 45 and 49).

items.title: The Fixed-term Employees (Prevention of Less Favourable Treatment) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Fixed-term Employees (Prevention of Less Favourable Treatment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2002 (“the 2002 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 4) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Claims and Payments Regulations”), the Social Security and Child Support (Decisions and Appeals) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1999 (“the Decisions and Appeals Regulations”), the Housing Benefit (Decisions and Appeals) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 (“the Housing Benefit Decisions and Appeals Regulations”), the Housing Benefit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (“the Housing Benefit Regulations”) and the Housing Benefit (Persons who have attained the qualifying age for state pension credit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (“the Housing Benefit State Pension Credit Regulations”).

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Motor Vehicles (Driving Licences) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (the “principal Regulations”) and implement in part Article 1(2) and Annex II of Commission Directive 2000/56/EC of 14th September 2000 amending Council Directive 91/439/EEC on driving licences.

items.title: The Child Maintenance (2008 Act) (Commencement No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order brings into operation provisions of the Child Maintenance Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 as follows:

items.title: The Condensed Milk and Dried Milk (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Condensed Milk and Dried Milk Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 (S.R. 2003 No. 300) (“the 2003 Regulations”). They make provision for the implementation of Council Directive 2007/61/EC amending Directive 2001/114/EC relating to certain partly or wholly dehydrated preserved milk for human consumption (OJ No.L258, 4.10.2007, p.27).

items.title: The Equipment and Protective Systems Intended for Use in Potentially Explosive Atmospheres (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Equipment and Protective Systems Intended for Use in Potentially Explosive Atmospheres Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (S.R. 1996 No. 247, as amended by S.R. 1998 No. 77 and S.R. 1999 No. 125) (“the principal Regulations”), which implemented Directive 94/9/EC of the European Parliament and the Council on the approximation of the laws of the Member States concerning equipment and protective systems intended for use in potentially explosive atmospheres (the ATEX Directive), to give effect to corrigenda to the ATEX Directive (O.J. No. L21, 26.1.2000, p.42 and O.J. No. L304, 5.12.2000, p.19) which makes typographical changes (regulation 3(3), (5), (8) and (10) to (16)).

items.title: The Optical Charges and Payments (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Health and Personal Social Services (Optical Charges and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made, by means of a voucher system, in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging for Supply) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations correct certain errors in the Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging for Supply) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2002 (S.R. 2002 No. 301, as amended by S.R. 2005 No. 165, S.R. 2005 No. 463, S.R. 2006 No. 173, S.R. 2006 No. 425, S.I. 2007/386 and S.I. 2008/2108) (“the principal Regulations”) in transposing provisions of—Council Directive 1992/32/EEC (O.J. No. L154, 5.6.92, p.1) amending for the 7th time Council Directive 67/548/EEC (O.J. No. L196, 16.8.67, p.1) on the approximation of the laws, regulations and administrative provisions relating to the classification, packaging and labelling of dangerous substances; andCouncil Directive 1999/45/EC (O.J. No. L200, 30.7.99, p.1) on the classification, packaging and labelling of dangerous preparations (“the preparations Directive”), concerning the labelling of single receptacles and receptacles in outer packagings where the national and international transport rules also apply.

items.title: The Welfare Reform (2007 Act) (Commencement No. 7) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Article 2 of this Order provides for the coming into operation of section 13(7) of the Welfare Reform Act (Northern Ireland) 2007 (“the Act”) on 27th October 2008.

items.title: The Health and Social Services Trusts (Establishment) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order amends orders made under Article 10(1) of The Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1991 establishing named Health and Social Services Trusts. Article 2 of the Order removes references to the establishments from which the relevant trusts carry out their functions and amends the Schedule. Article 3 removes the references to the Ambulance Control Centres from which the Northern Ireland Ambulance Service Health and Social Services Trust manages ambulance, patients’ transport and communications services.

items.title: Juvenile Justice Centre Rules (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Rules revoke the Juvenile Justice Centre Rules (Northern Ireland) 1999 and make provision for the management and discipline of juvenile justice centres.

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 5) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend various provisions relating to social security.

items.title: The Employment and Support Allowance (Transitional Provisions) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 2 of the Employment and Support Allowance (Transitional Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (“the principal Regulations”), which relate to the introduction of employment and support allowance. Regulation 2(1) of the principal Regulations sets out the general rule that a claim for incapacity benefit, severe disablement allowance or income support on the grounds of disability in respect of a period beginning on or after 27 October 2008 is to be treated as a claim for an employment and support allowance. Regulation 2(2) of the principal Regulations lists exceptions to this general rule.

items.title: Conservation of Scallops Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This version of the Conservation of Scallops Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Products of Animal Origin (Disease Control) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations partially transpose in Northern Ireland Articles 3 and 4 of Council Directive 2002/99/EC laying down the animal health rules governing the production, processing, distribution and introduction of products of animal origin for human consumption (OJ No L 18, 23.1.2003, p. 11).

items.title: Lands Tribunal (Salaries) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order provides for changes in the annual salaries payable to members of the Lands Tribunal for Northern Ireland following recommendations made in the Report of the Review Body on Senior Salaries in 2008 (HMSO CM 7388).

items.title: The Pesticides (Maximum Residue Levels) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations enforce the provisions of Regulation (EC) No 396/2005 of the European Parliament and of the Council on maximum residue levels of pesticides in or on food and feed of plant and animal origin and amending Council Directive 91/41/EEC (“Regulation 396/2005”).

items.title: The Health and Personal Social Services (Primary Medical Services Performers Lists) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These regulations make amendments to the Health and Personal Social services (Primary Medical Services Performers Lists) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2004 (“principal Regulations”), which provide for lists (“primary medical services performers lists”) of medical practitioners who may perform primary medical services for which Boards are under a duty to provide or secure the provision of.

items.title: The Organic Farming (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Organic Farming Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 by amending the interpretation of “the Council Regulation” to take account of the introduction of Council Regulation (EEC) No 834/2007 of 28 June 2007, which repeals and replaces Council Regulation (EEC) No 2092/91 from 1 January 2009.

items.title: The Public Health (Aircraft) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Public Health (Aircraft) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1971.

items.title: Police Service of Northern Ireland and Police Service of Northern Ireland Reserve (Full-Time) (Severance) (Amendment) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the definition of “compulsory retirement age” for the voluntary early retirement scheme for the Police Service of Northern Ireland and the Police Service of Northern Ireland Reserve (Full-Time). These Regulations have effect from 1 October 2006. This is possible because Articles 14 and 15 of the Superannuation (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 provide for regulations to have effect before they are made.

items.title: The Plant Health (Amendment No.2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Plant HealthOrder (Northern Ireland) 2006 (S.I. 2006/82) (“the principal Order”) so as to implement—

items.title: Pensions Appeal Tribunals (Northern Ireland) (Amendment) Rules 2008
items.description: These Rules amend the Pensions Appeal Tribunals (Northern Ireland) Rules 1981 to provide the procedure for a new route of appeal to the Upper Tribunal from a decision of the Pensions Appeal Tribunal in an assessment appeal.

items.title: The Insolvency (Company Arrangement or Administration Provisions for an Industrial and Provident Society) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Part II of the Insolvency (Northern Ireland) Order 1989 provides a regime for a company to enter into a voluntary arrangement with its creditors. Part III of the same Order provides for a regime for the administration of companies.

items.title: The Forced Marriage (Civil Protection) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order provides for the main provisions of the Forced Marriage (Civil Protection) Act 2007 relating to Northern Ireland to come into force on 25th November 2008.

items.title: The Cycle Routes (Amendment No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
The Social Security (Industrial Injuries) (Prescribed Diseases) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Industrial Injuries) (Prescribed Diseases) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1986, which prescribe diseases for which industrial injuries benefit is payable, by adding the disease nasopharyngeal cancer, as it relates to occupations involving working with wood, to the list of prescribed diseases in Part I of Schedule 1.

items.title: The Eggs and Chicks (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and remake, with modifications, the Eggs and Chicks Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (S.R. 2008 No. 98) following the adoption of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1234/2007 establishing a common organisation of agricultural markets and on specific provisions for certain agricultural products (Single CMO Regulation) (O.J. No. L 299, 16.11.2007, p. 1) and two Commission Regulations adopted under that Regulation, Commission Regulation (EC) No. 617/2008 (O.J. No. L168, 28.6.2008, p. 5) and Commission Regulation (EC) No. 589/2008 (O.J. No. L163, 24.6.2008, p. 6).

items.title: The Prison and Young Offenders Centre (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Rules amend the Prison and Young Offenders Centre Rules (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the principal rules”).

items.title: The Student Fees (Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Student Fees (Amounts) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”) by increasing the basic and higher amounts prescribed in the 2005 Regulations. The amendments come into operation on 1st September 2009.

items.title: The Scrapie (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the Department to charge a fee to meet the costs incurred by it for activities required, as regards scrapie, under point 1(a)(ii) of Part I of Chapter A of Annex VIII to the Community TSE Regulation which specifies the conditions for intra-Community trade in live animals, semen and embryos.

items.title: Victims of Mentally Disordered Offenders Information (Northern Ireland) Scheme 2008
items.description: This scheme is made under section 69A of the Justice (Northern Ireland) Act 2002. The purpose of the scheme is to put in place a mechanism whereby if an offence results in the making of a hospital order with a restriction order, or a transfer direction with a restriction direction in respect of the offender, the victim of that offence should have access to information about the offender’s discharge from hospital, or grant of leave of absence from hospital, and information as to any relevant conditions to which the offender is to be made subject in the event of the offender’s discharge or leave of absence from hospital. The scheme also provides for the victim to make representations to the Secretary of State about whether the offender should be subject to any conditions in the event of discharge or grant of leave of absence from hospital and if so, what conditions.

items.title: The Mutual Recognition of Driving Disqualifications (Northern Ireland and Ireland) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations are made under Part 3 of the Crime (International Co-operation) Act 2003 (“the Act”), which provides for mutual recognition of driving disqualifications between Member States of the European Union. Part 3 of the Act will be commenced in relation to Ireland on a date to be appointed, which will be advertised in the London, Edinburgh and Belfast Gazettes. The commencement date will also be notified on the Department of the Environment website.

items.title: Departments (Transfer of Functions) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order transfers certain functions of the Department of Culture, Arts and Leisure and the Minister of that Department to the Department of Finance and Personnel and the Minister of that Department respectively.

items.title: The Social Security (Incapacity Benefit Work-focused Interviews) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations remake the Social Security (Incapacity Benefit Work-focused Interviews) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”) with certain changes to align them, where possible, with the work-focused interview regime prescribed in Chapters 2 and 3 of Part 8 of the Employment and Support Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008.

items.title: The Family Proceedings (Amendment No. 3) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Rules amend the Family Proceedings Rules (Northern Ireland) 1996 (S.R. 1996 No. 322) (“the 1996 Rules”) to make provision for applications for a forced marriage protection order under Schedule 1 to the Forced Marriage (Civil Protection) Act 2007.

items.title: The Employment Rights (Increase of Limits) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order increases, from 2nd March 2008, the limits applying to certain awards of industrial tribunals, and other amounts payable under employment legislation, as specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2004 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 2nd February 2009 provisions of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2004 dealing with the provisions of live links in criminal proceedings.

items.title: Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 8th December 2008 provisions of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 dealing with the test purchasing of alcohol and pre charge bail provisions.

items.title: The Less Favoured Area Compensatory Allowances (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations provide for the implementation of Articles 13(a), 14(1), 14(2) first and second indents, and 15 of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1257/1999 (O.J. No. L160, 26.06.1999, p. 80) together with Articles 36(a)(ii) and 51(1) of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1698/2005 (O.J. No. L277, 21.10.2005, p. 1), in so far as those Council Regulations relate to less favoured areas.

items.title: The Christmas Bonus (Relevant Week) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order provides that for the year 2008 the relevant week for the purposes of Part X (Christmas bonus) of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 is the week beginning with Monday 22nd December 2008.

items.title: Fisheries (Amendment No. 2) Byelaws (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Byelaws amend the Fisheries Byelaws (Northern Ireland) 2003.

items.title: Eel Fishing (Licence Duties) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the licence duties payable to the Fisheries Conservancy Board for Northern Ireland in respect of licences for the use of fishing engines for the taking of eels. The new duties and the size of the increases, are set out below.

items.title: The Police Service of Northern Ireland (Promotion) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Royal Ulster Constabulary (Promotion) Regulations 1995 (S.R. 1995/120) (“the 1995 Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Lone Parents and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Income Support Regulations”), the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations”), the Social Security (Work-focused Interviews for Lone Parents) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 (“the Lone Parents Regulations”), the Social Security (Work-focused Interviews) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 (“the Work-focused Interviews Regulations”) and the Employment and Support Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008.

items.title: The Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) (Amendment No.3) 2008
items.description: These Rules amend the Rules of the Supreme Court (Northern Ireland) 1980 (“the principal Rules”) by—

items.title: The Rates (Regional Rates) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order fixes the amounts of the regional rates for the year ending 31st March 2009. It fixes 29.89 pence in the pound as the amount of the regional rate to be levied on the rateable net annual values of hereditaments and 0.3608 pence in the pound as the amount of the regional rate to be levied on the rateable capital values of hereditaments.

items.title: The Fair Employment (Specification of Public Authorities) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Fair Employment (Specification of Public Authorities) Order (Northern Ireland) 2004 (“the 2004 Order”) which specifies a number of persons or bodies as public authorities for certain purposes under Part VII of the Fair Employment and Treatment (Northern Ireland) Order 1998 (“the 1998 Order”).

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Marking and Identification of Passive Fishing Gear and Beam Trawls) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order provides for the enforcement of Community restrictions and other obligations relating to the marking and identification of passive gear and beam trawls deployed by fishing vessels as set out in Commission Regulation (EC) No. 356/2005, of 2 March 2005, p.8, OJ L56, as amended. In the context of this Order passive gear means longlines, gillnets, entangling nets, trammel nets, drifting gillnets and any associated anchoring, floating or navigational gear.

items.title: The Occupational Pensions (Revaluation) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2008/3070) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under paragraph 2(1) of Schedule 3 to the Pension Schemes Act 1993, specifies appropriate revaluation percentages. The percentages specified are relevant to the revaluation of benefits under occupational pension schemes, as required by section 80 of, and Schedule 2 to, the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993.

items.title: The Charges for Drugs and Appliances (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Charges for Drugs and Appliances Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the principal Regulations”), which provide for the making and recovery of charges for drugs and appliances supplied by doctors and chemists providing pharmaceutical services, and by hospitals and HSS trusts to out-patients.

items.title: The Child Maintenance (2008 Act) (Commencement No. 5) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Article 2 of this Order brings into operation provisions of Part 1 of the Child Maintenance Act (Northern Ireland) 2008. Part 1 sets out additional functions of the Department for Social Development following the passing of the Act, such as the promotion of child maintenance, and the provision of information and guidance to parents.

items.title: The Rates (Industrial Hereditaments) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order provides that for each of the financial years beginning on 1st April 2008, 1st April 2009 and 1st April 2010 the rateable net annual value of an industrial hereditament, where the hereditament is used wholly for industrial purposes, shall be 30 per cent. of its net annual value rather than, in the case of the financial year beginning on 1st April 2008, 50 per cent., and, in the case of the financial years beginning on 1st April 2009 and 1st April 2010, 75 per cent. of its net annual value. This means that rates will be chargeable in each of those financial years on 30% of the hereditament’s net annual value. Where an industrial hereditament is used partly for industrial purposes, 30 per cent. of the net annual value apportioned by the Commissioner of Valuation for Northern Ireland or the district valuer to the occupation and use of the hereditament for industrial purposes shall be applied in computing the rateable net annual value of the hereditament for each of those financial years.

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 6) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations make provision consequential upon the introduction of employment and support allowance by Part 1 of the Welfare Reform Act (Northern Ireland) 2007 (“the 2007 Act”) and lump sum payments in respect of diffuse mesothelioma by the Mesothelioma, etc., Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (“the 2008 Act”).

items.title: The Explosives (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations make minor amendments to 3 sets of Explosives Regulations.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of ... Measures) (Penalty Notices) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order creates a scheme of penalty notices for certain fisheries offences.

items.title: The Plant Health (Amendment No.3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Plant Health Order (Northern Ireland) 2006 (S.I. 2006/82) (“the principal Order”) so as to implement Commission Decision 2008/840/EC of 7th November 2008, putting in place certain import restrictions on plants, other than seeds, of Acer spp., intended for planting, originating in any third country so as to prevent the introduction into Northern Ireland of Anoplophora chinensis (Forster) (the citrus longhorn beetle).

items.title: The Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 (Retention and Disposal of Seized Motor Vehicles) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Under Article 180C of the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1981 (“the 1981 Order”) (retention etc. of vehicles seized under Article 180B) the police have certain powers relating to the removal etc. of vehicles seized under Article 180B of that Order. These Regulations provide for the retention, safe keeping and disposal by the police or persons authorised by them, of vehicles seized under those powers.

items.title: The Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 (Retention and Disposal of Seized Motor Vehicles) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Under Article 66 of the Criminal Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 (“the 2008 Order”) (retention etc. of vehicles seized under Article 65) the police have certain powers relating to the removal etc. of vehicles seized under Article 65 of that Order. These Regulations provide for the retention, safe keeping and disposal by the police or persons authorised by them, of vehicles seized under those powers.

items.title: The Seed Potatoes (Levy) (Revocation) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order provides for the revocation of certain Orders which provided for the payment of a levy to the Department of Agriculture and Rural Development by persons engaged by way of business in the production and marketing of seed potatoes.

items.title: The Social Security (Child Benefit Disregard) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 and the Housing Benefit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 so as to disregard in the calculation of income the increase in child benefit which takes effect on 5th January 2009. The Regulations cease to have effect at the beginning of April 2009, by which time it is anticipated that personal allowances will be increased by the annual up-rating order.

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments No. 7) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend—

items.title: Plant Protection Products (Amendment No 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Plant Protection Products Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 (S.R. 2005 No. 526) (“the principal Regulations”) which implement in Northern Ireland Council Directive 91/414/EEC (O.J. No. L230, 19.8.91, p1) to be read with the Corrigenda published in O.J. No. L170, 25.6.92, p. 40) concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market (“the Directive”), as amended.

items.title: The Teachers’ Pensions (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Teachers’ Superannuation (Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 and also the Teachers’ Superannuation Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1998.

items.title: The European Fisheries Fund (Grants) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations correct a drafting error in The European Fisheries Fund (Grants) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008. Those Regulations provide that the Department of Agriculture and Rural Development may pay grants for the purposes of Title IV of Council Regulation (EC) 1198/2006 on the European Fisheries Fund.

items.title: The Disabled Persons (Badges for Motor Vehicles) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Disabled Persons (Badges for Motor Vehicles) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993.

items.title: The Christmas Bonus (Specified Sum) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order provides that for the year 2008 the sum which is payable by way of Christmas bonus under section 144 of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 is increased from £10 to £70.

items.title: The Social Security (Housing Costs Special Arrangements) (Amendment and Modification) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend and modify the housing costs provisions in the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Income Support Regulations”), the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (“the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations”), the State Pension Credit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 (“the State Pension Credit Regulations”) and the Employment and Support Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (“the Employment and Support Allowance Regulations”).

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 and the Housing Benefit (Persons who have attained the qualifying age for state pension credit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006.

items.title: The Crown Court (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This version of the The Crown Court (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2008 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Executive Determinations) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit (Executive Determinations) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008.

items.title: Electricity (Guarantees of Origin of Electricity Produced from Renewable Energy Sources) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Electricity (Guarantees of Origin of Electricity Produced from Renewable Energy Sources ) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 which provide for certificates guaranteeing the origin of electricity generated from renewable sources to be issued to generators by the Northern Ireland Authority for Utility Regulation on request.

items.title: The Transmissible Spongiform Encephalopathies Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and remake with amendments The Transmissible Spongiform Encephalopathies Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (S.R. 2006 No. 202 as amended), which enforced Regulation (EC) No 999/2001 of the European Parliament and of the Council laying down rules for the prevention, control and eradication of certain transmissible spongiform encephalopathies (O.J. No. L 147, 31.5.2001, p.1) as amended (“the Community TSE Regulation”). These Regulations implement Commission Decision 2007/411 prohibiting the placing on the market of products derived from bovine animals born or reared within the United Kingdom before 1st August 1996 for any purpose and exempting such animals from certain control and eradication measures laid down in Regulation (EC) No. 999/2001 and repealing Decision 2005/598 (O.J. No. L 155, 15.6.2007, p.74). The Regulations also revoke The Bovine Products (Restriction on Placing on the Market) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 (S.R. 2005 No. 515 as amended).

items.title: The Social Security (National Insurance Numbers) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Crediting and Treatment of Contributions, and National Insurance Numbers) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 (“the 2001 Regulations”). The amendments are consequential on the coming into force of the Immigration (Restrictions on Employment) Order 2007 (“the 2007 Order”). The 2007 Order replaces the Immigration (Restrictions on Employment) Order 2004 (S.I. 2004/755) which listed the documents required to accompany an application for a national insurance number to be allocated under regulation 9(1A) of the 2001 Regulations.

items.title: Sexual Offences (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 (Commencement) Order 2008
items.description: This Order commences the Sexual Offences (Northern Ireland) Order 2008 as from the 2nd February 2009. Part 1 and Articles 80 and 82 of the 2008 Order came into operation one week after the day on which that Order was made.

items.title: Institutions of Further Education (Public Sector Audit) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order substitutes Article 19 of the Further Education (NI) Order 1997 to make provision for the Comptroller and Auditor General for Northern Ireland to audit the annual accounts of the governing body of an institution of further education with effect from the 2007/08 annual accounts. The Order also places a duty on the Department for Employment and Learning to lay a copy of the statement of accounts and of the Comptroller and Auditor General’s Report before the Assembly.

items.title: The Measuring Instruments (Use for Trade) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Regulations specified in the Schedule by inserting in each of them a definition of “member State” so as to expressly include Norway, Iceland and Liechtenstein in that definition. This means that when an instrument to which any of those Regulations relate is made available in any of these countries it is placed on the market for the purposes of those Regulations.

items.title: The Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (S.R. 2006 No. 401) (the “principal Regulations”), to take account of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1266/2007 on implementing rules for Council Directive 2000/75/EC as regards the control, monitoring, surveillance and restrictions on movements of certain animals of susceptible species in relation to bluetongue.

items.title: Electricity (Offshore Wind and Water Driven Generating Stations) (Permitted Capacity) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Article 39 of the Electricity (Northern Ireland) Order 1992 No. 232 N.I. 1 provides that a generating station with a capacity of more than 10 megawatts (“the permitted capacity”) shall not be constructed, extended or operated except in accordance with a consent granted by the Department of Enterprise, Trade and Investment (“the Department”).

items.title: The Offshore Electricity Development (Environmental Impact Assessment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations are concerned with the implementation in Northern Ireland of Council Directive 85/337/EEC as amended by Council Directive 97/11/EC on the assessment of the effects of certain public and private developments on the environment (“the Directive”).

items.title: Police Service of Northern Ireland (Conduct) (Amendment) Regulations 2008
items.description: These regulations amend the Royal Ulster Constabulary (Conduct) Regulations 2000 and the Royal Ulster Constabulary (Conduct) (Senior officer) Regulations 2000 to introduce the Code of Ethics 2008.

items.title: The Bluetongue (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Bluetongue Order (Northern Ireland) 2008 to bring that Order into operation on the 16th February 2008.

items.title: The Plant Health (Import Inspection Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Plant Health (Import Inspection Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 (S.R. 2005/373) (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations implement Article 13d of Council Directive 2000/29/EC (OJ No. L169, 10.7.00, P.1) as amended by Council Directive 2002/89/EC (O.J. No. L355, 30.12.02, P.45-60), which requires Member States to charge fees to cover the costs occasioned by the documentary checks, identity checks and plant health checks of certain imports of plants, plant products and other objects from third countries. These minimum fees are revised annually by the EC.

items.title: The Traffic Signs (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Traffic Signs Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997.

items.title: The Pensions (2008 Act) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of section 15 of, and Schedule 5 and Part 8 of Schedule 6 to, the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (provision relating to the removal of the Department’s role in approving actuarial guidance) on 29th February 2008.

items.title: The Domestic Energy Efficiency Grants (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: The Domestic Energy Efficiency Grants Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2002 (“the 2002 Regulations”) enable the Department for Social Development to make or arrange for the making of grant for the improvement of energy efficiency in dwellings occupied by persons on low incomes with children, elderly persons on low incomes and persons in receipt of benefit relating to ill health or disability.

items.title: The Rate Relief (Qualifying Age) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Rate Relief (Qualifying Age) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for rate relief for claimants who have attained the qualifying age for state pension credit provided the claimant or if he has a partner, the claimant’s partner, is not in receipt of income support or an income-based jobseeker’s allowance.

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Payments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This version of the The Social Security (Claims and Payments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: Dairy Produce Quotas (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations, which come into operation on 1st April 2008, amend the Dairy Produce Quotas Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005, (S.R. 2005 No.70) (“the 2005 Regulations”).

items.title: The Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers’ Compensation) (Payment of Claims) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Under the Pneumoconiosis, etc., (Workers’ Compensation) (Northern Ireland) Order 1979 (“the Order”) lump sum payments may be made to certain persons disabled by a disease to which the Order applies, or to dependants of persons who were so disabled before they died.

items.title: The Waste (Amendment) (2007 Order) (Commencement No.2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order brings Article 10 of the Waste (Amendment) (Northern Ireland) Order 2007 (“the 2007 Order”) into operation on 7th April 2008.

items.title: The Conduct of Employment Agencies and Employment Businesses (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Conduct of Employment Agencies and Employment Businesses Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”).

items.title: The Producer Responsibility Obligations (Packaging Waste) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Producer Responsibility Obligations (Packaging Waste) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (the “2007 Regulations”). These Regulations correct an error in the 2007 Regulations in respect of the definition of the Waste Directive. The 2007 Regulations impose on producers the obligation to recover and recycle packaging waste and related obligations, in order to attain the recovery and recycling targets set out in Article 6(1) of Council Directive 94/62/EC on packaging and packaging waste (OJ No. L 365, 31.12.1994, p.10, as amended by Council Regulations (EC) No. 1882/2003 (OJ No. L 284, 31.10.2003, p.1), Council Directive 2004/12/EC (OJ No. L 47, 18.2.2004, p.26) and Council Directive 2005/20/EC (OJ No. L 70, 16.3.2005, p.17)). These Regulations increase the recovery and recycling targets imposed on producers by the 2007 Regulations.

items.title: The Penalty Charges (Additional Contravention) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 1 to the Traffic Management (Northern Ireland) Order 2005 (“the 2005 Order”) by adding a further offence under Article 50 of the Road Traffic (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 of contravening in respect of a stationary vehicle a traffic sign indicating a prohibition on the use of Royal Avenue and part of Rosemary Street, Belfast, Dunluce Street, Larne, North Street, Carrickfergus and Church Lane, Portadown.

items.title: The Special Educational Needs and Disability (General Qualifications Bodies) (Relevant Qualifications, Reasonable Steps and Physical Features) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the relevant qualifications in respect of which it is unlawful for a general qualifications body to discriminate against a disabled person, (Regulation 2 and the Schedule).

items.title: The Disability Discrimination (Private Clubs, etc.) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations modify the duties imposed on private clubs and other associations by sections 21F to 21J of the Disability Discrimination Act 1995 (“the 1995 Act”), as inserted by the Disability Discrimination (Northern Ireland) Order 2006, and impose a duty on them to make adjustments.

items.title: The Meat Products (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Meat Products Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2004 (S.R. 2004 No. 13, as amended).

items.title: Pig Production Development (Levy) Revocation (No 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order revokes the Pig Production Development (Levy) Order (NI) 1996 consequential upon the dissolution of the Pig Production Development Committee (by S.R. 2007 No. 354). The Committee is dissolved and liability for levy ceases on 31 March 2008.

items.title: The Guaranteed Minimum Pensions Increase Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2008/581) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under section 109(4) of the Pension Schemes Act 1993, specifies 3 per cent. as the percentage by which that part of any guaranteed minimum pension attributable to earnings factors for the tax years 1988-1989 to 1996-1997, and payable by contracted-out, defined benefit occupational pension schemes, is to be increased.

items.title: Plant Protection Products (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Plant Protection Products Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 (S.R. 2005 No. 526) (“the principal Regulations”) which implement in Northern Ireland Council Directive 91/414/EEC (O.J. No. L230, 19.8.91, p. 1 to be read with the Corrigenda published in O.J. No. L170, 25.6.92, p. 40) concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market (“the Directive”), as amended.

items.title: The Social Security Pensions (Home Responsibilities) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security Pensions (Home Responsibilities) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1994.

items.title: The Meat (Official Controls Charges) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Meat (Official Controls Charges) (No.2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007 (S.R. 2007 No. 496).

items.title: The Recovery of Health Services Charges (Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: The Recovery of Health Services Charges (Northern Ireland) Order 2006 provides for a scheme for the recovery of charges in cases where an injured person who receives a compensation payment in respect of his injury has received health services hospital treatment or ambulance services. The charges are specified in certificates issued by the Department for Social Development and are payable by persons who pay compensation to the injured person. The charges in each case are calculated by reference to a tariff of charges provided for by the Recovery of Health Services Charges (Amounts) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Health and Personal Social Services (Assessment of Resources) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
