items.description: These Regulations make further amendments to the Health and Personal Social Services (Assessment of Resources) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993 (“the principal regulations”) which relate to the assessment by Health and Social Services Boards and HSC Trusts of the resources of residents in accommodation arranged under Articles 15 and 36 of the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972.

items.title: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2008/632) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under sections 150 and 150A of the Social Security Administration Act 1992, increases the rates and amounts of certain social security benefits and other sums.

items.title: The Welfare Reform (2007 Act) (Commencement No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of provisions of the Welfare Reform Act (Northern Ireland) 2007 relating to housing benefit as follows—

items.title: The Insolvency (Disqualification from Office: General) Order (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: Individuals who are bankrupt may be prevented by certain statutory provisions (“disqualification provisions”) from being elected or appointed to, or holding, an office or position, or from becoming or remaining a member of a body or group. Article 24 of the Insolvency (Northern Ireland) Order 2005 confers a power to amend such a disqualification provision so that as well as, or instead of, it applying to individuals who are bankrupt it applies to individuals who are subject to a bankruptcy restrictions regime.

items.title: Certification Officer (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations prescribe and revise fees payable in respect of certain matters to the Certification Officer under the Industrial Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1992 (“the 1992 Order”) and the Trade Union and Labour Relations (Northern Ireland) Order 1995 (“the 1995 Order”). The fees were previously prescribed in the Certification Officer (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 (“the 1997 regulations”). In addition the regulations provide for the introduction of a new fee for the issue of a certificate of independence to an amalgamated trade union.

items.title: The Health and Personal Social Services (Superannuation Scheme and Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Health and Personal Social Services (Superannuation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (“the Superannuation Scheme Regulations”), which make provision for the superannuation of HPSS employees, and the Health and Personal Social Services (Compensation for Premature Retirement) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1983 (“the Compensation for Premature Retirement Regulations”), which provide for the payment of compensation to or in respect of a person who was a HPSS employee and has prematurely retired from that employment.

items.title: The Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes (General Levy) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Occupational and Personal Pension Schemes (General Levy) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”).

items.title: The Eggs and Chicks Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations revoke and remake, with modifications, the Eggs (Marketing Standards) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1995 No. 382).

items.title: The Rice Products from the United States of America (Restriction on First Placing on the Market) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement Commission Decision 2006/601/EC on emergency measures regarding the non-authorised genetically modified organism “LL RICE 601” in rice products (OJ No. L 244, 7.9.2006, p.27) as last amended by Commission Decision 2008/162/EC amending Decision 2006/601/EC on emergency measures regarding the non-authorised genetically modified organism “LL RICE 601” in rice products (OJ No.L52, 27.2.2008, p.25). They revoke and re-enact the Rice Products (Restriction on First Placing on the Market) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (S.R. 2006 No. 443).

items.title: The Individual Learning Account (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 31st January 2008 amend the Individual Learning Account (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (“the principal regulations”). The principal regulations specify arrangements which qualify under section 2 of the Education and Training (Scotland) Act 2000 and provide for the payment of grants in respect of persons who are parties to the qualifying arrangements.

items.title: The Shrimp Fishing Nets (Scotland) Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends one of the specified exceptions to the prohibition against carrying or deploying a net with a mesh size of between 16 and 31 millimetres provided for in article 3 of the Shrimp Fishing Nets (Scotland) Order 2004.

items.title: The Rural Development Contracts (Rural Priorities) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations introduce measures to supplement Council Regulation (EC) No. 1698/2005 (“the Council Regulation”) which lays down general rules governing Community support for rural development (financed by the European Agricultural Fund for Rural Development established by Council Regulation (EC) No. 1290/2005 (O.J. No. L 209, 11.8.2005, p.1.)) and Commission Regulations (EC) Nos. 1974/2006 and 1975/2006 laying down detailed rules for the application of the Council Regulation.

items.title: The Aquaculture and Fisheries (Scotland) Act 2007 (Fixed Penalty Notices) Order 2008
items.description: This Order makes provision for fixed penalty notices which are provided for in sections 25 to 31 of the Aquaculture and Fisheries (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”).

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Control Procedures for Herring, Mackerel and Horse Mackerel) (Scotland) Order 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order implements Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1542/2007 on landing and weighing procedures for herring, mackerel and horse mackerel (“the Commission Regulation”). In exercise of powers introduced by the Legislative and Regulatory Reform Act 2006, references to the Commission Regulation are to that instrument as it may be amended from time to time.

items.title: The Enforcement of Fines (Seizure and Disposal of Vehicles) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: Sections 226A to 226I of the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995 (“the 1995 Act”), inserted by section 55 of the Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007, make provision regarding the enforcement of “relevant penalties”, as defined in section 226I of the 1995 Act.

items.title: The Enforcement of Fines (Diligence) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: Sections 226A to 226I of the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995 (“the 1995 Act”) were inserted by section 55 of the Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007. These sections make provision regarding the enforcement of “relevant penalties”, as defined in section 226I of the 1995 Act.

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for the making and recovery of charges for drugs and appliances (other than dental or optical appliances) supplied under or by virtue of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978.

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Regulations 1998 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made by means of a voucher system, in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Budget (Scotland) Act 2007 Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Budget (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”), which makes provision for the financial year 2007/08, for the use of resources by the Scottish Administration and certain bodies whose expenditure is payable out of the Scottish Consolidated Fund, for authorising the use of resources, and for limits on the capital expenditure of and borrowing of local authorities and certain other public bodies. The 2007 Act was amended by the Budget (Scotland) Act 2007 Amendment Order 2007 (S.S.I. 2007/551).

items.title: The Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995 Fixed Penalty Order 2008
items.description: Section 50(1) of the Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 amends section 302 (fixed penalty: conditional offer by procurator fiscal) of the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995.

items.title: The Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (Supplemental Provisions) Order 2008
items.description: This Order makes amendments to the Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”) which are ancillary to the provisions of the 2007 Act.

items.title: The Bluetongue (Scotland) Order 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order continues to implement Council Directive 2000/75/EC (“the Directive”) laying down specific provisions for the control and eradication of bluetongue. It revokes and remakes the Bluetongue (Scotland) Order 2003 in order also to enforce Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1266/2007.

items.title: The Trunk Roads (Route A84) (Callander) (Prohibition of Waiting and Loading) (Variation) Order 2008
Act of Sederunt (Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules) Amendment (Adult Support and Protection (Scotland) Act 2007) 2008
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes amendments to Part XVI of the Summary Applications Rules (“the Rules”) in consequence of the Adult Support and Protection (Scotland) Act 2007 (asp 10).

items.title: The Scottish Commission for Human Rights Act 2006 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st April 2008 the remaining provisions of the Scottish Commission for Human Rights Act 2006 (“the Act”). This is the second Commencement Order. From 1st April 2008 the Act will be in force for all purposes.

items.title: The A90 Trunk Road (Oatyhill) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The A96 Trunk Road (West Road, Elgin) (Prohibition of Waiting) Order 2008
The Bankruptcy and Diligence etc. (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 3, Savings and Transitionals) Order 2008
items.description: Article 3 of this Order brings into force on 1st April 2008 provisions of the Bankruptcy and Diligence etc. (Scotland) Act 2007 (asp 3) (“the Act”), subject to transitional and saving provisions.

items.title: The Adult Support and Protection (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions) Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Adult Support and Protection (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 2 and Transitional Provisions) Order 2008 to clarify a potential ambiguity in the drafting of article 2(2) of that Order.

items.title: The Police (Special Constables) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Police (Special Constables) (Scotland) Regulations 1966 (“the 1966 Regulations”).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Shorthand Writers in the Sheriff Court) (Amendment) 2008
items.description: This Act of Sederunt increases the fees payable to shorthand writers in the Sheriff Court by 3.6%. The last increase was effected by the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Shorthand Writers in the Sheriff Court) (Amendment) 2007 (S.S.I. 2007/211).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Sheriff Court Bankruptcy Rules) 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Act of Sederunt restates and amends the rules in the sheriff court in relation to the Bankruptcy (Scotland) Act 1985 as a result of the amendments made to that Act by the Bankruptcy and Diligence etc. (Scotland) Act 2007.

items.title: The Condensed Milk and Dried Milk (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, implement Council Directive 2007/61/EC by amending the Condensed Milk and Dried Milk (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (“the Principal Regulations”).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 2) (Fees of Shorthand Writers) 2008
items.description: This Act of Sederunt increases the fees payable to shorthand writers in the Court of Session by 3.6%. The last increase was effected by the Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 3) (Fees of Shorthand Writers) 2007 (S.S.I. 2007/234).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Sheriff Court Rules Amendment) (Diligence) 2008
items.description: (This note is not part of the Act of Sederunt)

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 3) (Bankruptcy and Diligence etc. (Scotland) Act 2007) 2008
items.description: (This note is not part of the Act of Sederunt)

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 4) (Miscellaneous) 2008
items.description: (This note is not part of the Act of Sederunt)

items.title: The Home Detention Curfew Licence (Prescribed Standard Conditions) (Scotland) Revocation Order 2008
items.description: This Order revokes the Home Detention Curfew Licence (Prescribed Standard Conditions) (Scotland) Order 2008 which prescribes standard conditions to be included in any licence issued in respect of long-term or short-term prisoners released on licence under section 3AA(1) of the Prisoners and Criminal Proceedings (Scotland) Act 1993.

items.title: The Home Detention Curfew Licence (Prescribed Standard Conditions) (Scotland) (No. 2) Order 2008
items.description: This Order prescribes standard conditions for the purposes of section 12AA of the Prisoners and Criminal Proceedings (Scotland) Act 1993 (“the 1993 Act”).

items.title: The Home Detention Curfew Licence (Amendment of Specified Days) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the period specified in section 3AA(3) of the Prisoners and Criminal Proceedings (Scotland) Act 1993.

items.title: The Plastic Materials and Articles in Contact with Food (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Plastic Materials and Articles in Contact with Food (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2006 and re enact those Regulations with changes.

items.title: The Sexual Offences Act 2003 (Prescribed Police Stations) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: Section 87(1) of the Sexual Offences Act 2003 provides that a sex offender who is subject to the notification requirements in Part 2 of that Act fulfils their requirements by attending any police station in their local police area which is prescribed for this purpose in regulations.

items.title: The Eggs and Chicks (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which only extend to Scotland, revoke and replace, with modifications, the Eggs (Marketing Standards) (Enforcement) (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”).

items.title: The National Assistance (Assessment of Resources) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Assistance (Assessment of Resources) Regulations 1992 (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations concern the assessment of a person’s liability to pay for accommodation provided under the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 (“the 1968 Act”). By virtue of section 87(3) of the 1968 Act, accommodation provided under the 1968 Act or section 25 of the Mental Health (Care and Treatment) (Scotland) Act 2003 (asp 13) shall be regarded as accommodation provided under Part III of the National Assistance Act 1948.

items.title: The Adoption and Children (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings into force certain provisions of the Adoption and Children (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the Act”). Article 2(1) brings into force, on 7th April 2008, all the provisions of the Act listed in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Official Statistics (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order specifies that wholly Scottish devolved statistics produced, or to be produced, by the persons listed in Schedule 1 are official statistics. “Scottish devolved statistics” is defined in section 66 of the Statistics and Registration Service Act 2007.

items.title: The Renewables Obligation (Scotland) Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Renewables Obligation (Scotland) Order 2007.

items.title: The Housing Support Grant (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order fixes for the year 2008–2009 the aggregate amount of the housing support grants payable under section 191 of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 (article 2).

items.title: The A92 Trunk Road (B969 Western Avenue Junction, Glenrothes) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The Forestry Challenge Funds (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the implementation of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1698/2005 on support for rural development from the European Agricultural Fund for Rural Development and Commission Regulations (EC) Nos. 1974/2006 and 1975/2006 laying down detailed rules for the application of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1698/2005. The Regulations provide for funding being made available by the Forestry Commissioners from the Scotland Rural Development Programme 2007–13 for purposes related to forestry.

items.title: The Local Government Finance (Scotland) Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Local Government Finance (Scotland) Order 2008 by replacing Schedule 1 to that Order with the Schedule contained in this Order. Only the figures contained in column 2 of the Schedule are affected. Column 2 of that Schedule lists the amount of revenue support grant which is payable to each local authority in Scotland in respect of the financial year 2008–2009.

items.title: The Advice and Assistance (Financial Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations–

items.title: The Civil Legal Aid (Financial Conditions) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations increase certain financial limits of eligibility for civil legal aid under the Legal Aid (Scotland) Act 1986.

items.title: The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.3) Order 2008
The National Assistance (Sums for Personal Requirements) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: Section 22(4) of the National Assistance Act 1948 as applied by section 87(3) and (4) of the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 (“the 1968 Act”) requires a local authority to assume in assessing a person’s liability to pay for accommodation provided under the 1968 Act or section 25 of the Mental Health (Care and Treatment) (Scotland) Act 2003 (asp 13) that he or she will need for his or her personal requirements such sum per week as may be prescribed by regulations.

items.title: The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.3) Order 2008
The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 3) Order 2008
The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 3) Order 2008
The Protected Trust Deeds (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations are made under paragraph 5 of Schedule 1 to the Bankruptcy (Scotland) Act 1985, as amended by the Bankruptcy and Diligence etc (Scotland) Act 2007. They make provision about how trust deeds become protected, the consequences of a trust deed being granted that status, the rights of creditors, the discharge of the debtor and trustee from the trust deed, and the administration of trust deeds.

items.title: The Companies Act 2006 (Scottish public sector companies to be audited by the Auditor General for Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order provides that certain companies with registered offices in Scotland are to have their accounts audited by the Auditor General for Scotland. This means that in terms of section 475 of the Companies Act 2006 these companies will be exempt from the auditing of company accounts requirements of Part 16 of that Act. The companies subject to this Order are non-profit making public sector companies, which appear to Scottish Ministers in terms of section 483(2) of that Act to carry out functions of a public nature or are funded by bodies audited by the Auditor General for Scotland.

items.title: The A835 Trunk Road (Tore to Maryburgh Cycle Track) (Redetermination of Means of Exercise of Public Right of Passage) Order 2008
The M77/A77 Trunk Road (Bellfield to Dutchhouse) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2003 (“the Regulations”).

items.title: The Specified Products from China (Restriction on First Placing on the Market) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations which extend to Scotland only, implement Commission Decision 2008/289/EC on emergency measures regarding the unauthorised genetically modified organism  “Bt 63” in rice products.

items.title: The Management of Offenders etc. (Scotland) Act 2005 (Commencement No. 5) Order 2008
items.description: Article 2 of this Order brings into force certain provisions of the Management of Offenders etc. (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the 2005 Act”).

items.title: The Transport (Scotland) Act 2005 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2008
items.description: This Order appoints various days for the coming into force of provisions of the Transport (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the Act”). The provisions commenced relate to Part 2 on Road Works.

items.title: The Tribunals, Courts and Enforcement Act 2007 (Commencement) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings Part 6 (Protection of Cultural Objects on Loan) of the Tribunals, Courts and Enforcement Act 2007 into force on 21st April 2008 in relation to Scotland. Part 6 provides for the protection from seizure or forfeiture of cultural objects on loan from abroad for temporary public exhibition in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Enforcement of Community Quota and Third Country Fishing Measures and Restriction on Days at Sea) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order, which forms part of the law of Scotland only, makes provision for the enforcement of Council Regulation (EC) No. 40/2008 (“the Council Regulation”).

items.title: The Serious Crime Act 2007 (Commencement No. 1) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings into force on 28th April 2008 section 86 of the Serious Crime Act 2007. This amends section 387 of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (c. 29) (issue of search warrants in Scotland in connection with certain investigations) to provide that, in Scotland, a proper person may use reasonable force in executing a search warrant issued under that section where such force is necessary. “Proper person” is defined in section 412 of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002. It means a constable, in relation to a confiscation investigation or a money laundering investigation, or the Scottish Ministers or a person named by them, in relation to a civil recovery investigation. “Constable” is further defined in that section as including a customs and excise officer.

items.title: The Plant Health Fees (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972, consolidate the Plant Health Fees (Scotland) Regulations 1996, the Plant Health Fees (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2004, the Plant Health Fees (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2005 and the Plant Health Fees (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2007. The Regulations also prescribe increased fees in respect of inspections carried out for the purpose of considering an application for, or the continuing operation of an authority to issue plant passports under article 29 of the Plant Health (Scotland) Order 2005(“the 2005 Order”) or article 8 of the Plant Health (Phytophthora ramorum) (Scotland) Order 2004 (“the 2004 Order). Other fees are unchanged.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, consolidate the four sets of regulations which have previously declared fuels to be authorised fuels for the purposes of Part III of the Clean Air Act 1993. The authorised fuels are anthracite, semi - anthracite,  electricity, gas, low volatile steam coals and those described in Schedule 1.

items.title: The Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Animals and Animal Products (Import and Export) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the principal Regulations”), to take account of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1266/2007 on implementing rules for Council Directive 2000/75/EC as regards the control, monitoring, surveillance and restrictions on movements of certain animals of susceptible species in relation to bluetongue.

items.title: The Sea Fishing (Control Procedures for Herring, Mackerel and Horse Mackerel) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Sea Fishing (Control Procedures for Herring, Mackerel and Horse Mackerel) (Scotland) Order 2008 (“the principal Order”) which implemented Commission Regulation (EC) No. 1542/2007 (O.J. No. L 337, 21.12.2007, p.56 (“the Commission Regulation”)).

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempt Fireplaces) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order, which extends to Scotland only, makes provision for exemption from the prohibition, under section 20 of the Clean Air Act 1993, of the emission of smoke in smoke control areas.

items.title: The Products of Animal Origin (Disease Control) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order transposes in Scotland Articles 3 and 4 of Council Directive 2002/99/EC laying down the animal health rules governing the production, processing, distribution and introduction of products of animal origin for human consumption (O.J. No. L 18, 23.1.2003, p.11) (“the Council Directive”).

items.title: The Rural Development Contracts (Land Managers Options) (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations introduce measures to supplement Council Regulation (EC) No. 1698/2005 (“the Council Regulation”) which lays down general rules governing Community support for rural development (financed by the European Agricultural Fund for Rural Development established by Council Regulation (EC) No. 1290/2005 (O.J. No. L 209, 11.8.2005, p.1.)) and Commission Regulations (EC) Nos. 1974/2006 and 1975/2006 laying down detailed rules for the application of the Council Regulation.

items.title: The Scottish Road Works Register (Prescribed Fees and Amounts) Regulations 2008
items.description: Section 112A(1) of the New Roads and Street Works Act 1991 (“the Act”) (as inserted by section 19 of the Transport (Scotland) Act 2005) provides for the Scottish Road Works Commissioner (“the Commissioner”) to keep a register to be known as the Scottish Road Works Register (“the SRWR”).

items.title: The Firefighters' Pension Scheme (Scotland) Order 2007 Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Firefighters' Pension Scheme (set out in the Schedule to the Firefighter’s Pension Scheme (Scotland) Order 2007) (“the new Scheme”).

items.title: The Firefighters' Pension Scheme Amendment (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Firefighters' Pension Scheme (set out in Schedule 2 to the Firemen’s Pension Scheme Order 1992) as it has effect in Scotland (“the 1992 Scheme”).

items.title: The Land Managers Skills Development Grants (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations establish a framework for the payment in Scotland of grants towards training initiatives aimed at improving the skills of individuals involved in forestry or the management or use of agricultural land in Scotland.

items.title: The Designation of Institutions of Higher Education (The Scottish Agricultural College) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order designates The Scottish Agricultural College as an institution of higher education for the purpose of Part II of the Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 1992. It also removes the recognition of The Scottish Agricultural College as a central institution for the purpose of section 135(1) of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980 by revoking the Central Institutions (Recognition) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/2386).

items.title: The Planning etc. (Scotland) Act 2006 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings section 2 of the Planning etc. (Scotland) Act 2006 (“the Act”) into force on 19th May 2008 for the purposes of enabling orders and regulations to be made and directions and guidance to be issued. The Order brings sections 2 and 54 (17)(a)(i) of the Act into force for other purposes on 25th June 2008.

items.title: The Planning etc. (Scotland) Act 2006 (Development Planning) (Saving Provisions) Order 2008
items.description: This Order preserves Part 2 of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997 (“the 1997 Act”) in full force and effect notwithstanding the commencement for certain purposes of section 2 of the Planning etc. (Scotland) Act 2006 (“the 2006 Act”). Section 2 of the 2006 Act substitutes a new Part 2 for Part 2 of the 1997 Act.

items.title: The Transmissible Spongiform Encephalopathies (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Transmissible Spongiform Encephalopathies (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the principal Regulations”), which provide for the enforcement in Scotland of Regulation (EC) No. 999/2001 of the European Parliament and of the Council laying down rules for the prevention, control and eradication of certain transmissible spongiform encephalopathies (O.J. No. L 147, 31.5.2001, p.1) as amended by and as read with the Community instruments set out in Schedule 1 to the principal Regulations.

items.title: The A720 Trunk Road (Edinburgh City Bypass) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic, Temporary Prohibition of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restriction) Order 2008
The Lyon Court and Office Fees (Variation) (No. 2) Order 2008
items.description: This Order varies the fees payable in respect of devolved functions in Schedule B to the Lyon King of Arms Act 1867 by replacing them with the fees listed in the Schedule to this Order. The Lyon Court and Office Fees (Variation) Order 2008 varied the fees payable in respect of reserved functions in Schedule B by replacing them with the fees listed in the Schedule to that Order.

items.title: The A82 Trunk Road (Glencoe Village) (40mph Speed Limit) Order 2008
The Conservation (Natural Habitats, &c.) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, make further provision for the transposition of Council Directive 92/43/EEC on the conservation of natural habitats and of wild flora and fauna (“the Habitats Directive”) (O.J. No. L 206, 22.7.1992, p.7). In so doing they amend the Conservation (Natural Habitats, &c.) Regulations 1994 (“the 1994 Regulations”).

items.title: The Bathing Waters (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the purpose of implementing, in Scotland, Directive 2006/7/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council concerning the management of bathing water quality and repealing Directive 76/160/EEC (O.J. L 64, 4.3.2006, p.37).

items.title: The Schools (Health Promotion and Nutrition) (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2008
items.description: This Order appoints two days for the coming into force of all the provisions of the Schools (Health Promotion and Nutrition) (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the Act”) not already in force.

items.title: The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.4) Order 2008
The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.4) Order 2008
The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.4) Order 2008
The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.4) Order 2008
The Guar Gum (Restriction on First Placing on the Market) (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, implement Commission Decision 2008/352/EC imposing special conditions governing guar gum originating in or consigned from India due to contamination risk of those products by pentachlorophenal and dioxins (“the Commission Decision”).

items.title: The Designation of Institutions of Higher Education (The Scottish Agricultural College) (Scotland) (No. 2) Order 2008
items.description: Article 2 of this Order designates The Scottish Agricultural College as an institution of higher education for the purpose of Part II of the Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 1992.

items.title: The Central Institutions (Recognition) (Scotland) Revocation Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Central Institutions (Recognition) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”) as a consequence of the designation of The Scottish Agricultural College (the only remaining body recognised as a Central Institution under the 1990 Regulations) as an institution providing higher education for the purposes of the Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 1992 (c. 37). That designation is effected by the Designation of Institutions of Higher Education (The Scottish Agricultural College) (Scotland) (No. 2) Order 2008 (S.S.I. 2008/177).

items.title: The Justice of the Peace Courts (Sheriffdom of Grampian, Highland and Islands) Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order makes amendments to the Justice of the Peace Courts (Sheriffdom of Grampian, Highland and Islands) Order 2008 (“the 2008 Order”).

items.title: The A92/A972 Trunk Road (Cadham Road and Car Wash Facility) (Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The Food Labelling (Declaration of Allergens) (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations extend to Scotland only. They further amend the Food Labelling Regulations 1996 (“the principal regulations”) which extend to the whole of Great Britain.

items.title: The Mental Health (Cross-border Visits) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations make provision in connection with escorted mental health patients who visit Scotland whilst on leave of absence under the law of England and Wales, Northern Ireland, the Isle of Man or any of the Channel Islands (“a relevant territory”).

items.title: The Caledonian Maritime Assets Limited (Largs) Harbour Revision Order 2008
items.description: This version of the The Caledonian Maritime Assets Limited (Largs) Harbour Revision Order 2008 is the published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer for Scotland. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The A92/A972 Trunk Road (Kingsway East, Dundee) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The Common Agricultural Policy (Single Farm Payment and Support Schemes and Cross-Compliance) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Regulation (EC) No. 146/2008 which, among other things, amends Council Regulation (EC) No. 1782/2003 establishing common rules for direct support schemes under the common agricultural policy. They also implement Commission Regulation (EC) No. 319/2008, which amends Commission Regulation (EC) No. 795/2004 laying down detailed rules for the implementation of the single payment scheme provided for in Council Regulation (EC) No. 1782/2003, and Commission Regulation (EC) No. 796/2004 laying down detailed rules for the implementation of cross-compliance, modulation and the integrated administration and control system provided for in Council Regulation (EC) No. 1782/2003.

items.title: The Victim Notification Scheme (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends section 16(1) and (3) of the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”).

items.title: The Public Transport Users' Committee for Scotland Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Public Transport Users' Committee for Scotland Order 2006 to specify a range for the number of members of the Committee of 10 to 15 (including the convener) and to ensure that 3 of those members will be disabled persons. These amendments are made to reflect the fact that the Committee will now consider matters formerly considered by the Mobility and Access Committee for Scotland.

items.title: The Mobility and Access Committee for Scotland Revocation Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Mobility and Access Committee for Scotland Regulations 2002 and the Mobility and Access Committee for Scotland Amendment Regulations 2005. Matters considered by that Committee are now to be considered by the Public Transport Users' Committee for Scotland.

items.title: The Dumfries and Galloway Council (Port William) Harbour Empowerment Order 2008
items.description: This Order establishes Dumfries and Galloway Council as the harbour authority for the harbour of Port William. The Order confers duties and powers on the Council as harbour authority, and makes related provisions.

items.title: The Dumfries and Galloway Council (Isle of Whithorn) Harbour Empowerment Order 2008
items.description: This Order establishes Dumfries and Galloway Council as the harbour authority for the harbour of Isle of Whithorn. The Order confers duties and powers on the Council as harbour authority, and makes related provisions.

items.title: The Annual Close Time (Permitted Periods of Fishing) (River Dee (Aberdeenshire) Salmon Fishery District) Order 2008
items.description: The annual close time of the River Dee (Aberdeenshire) salmon fishery district is 27th August to 10th February. Section 37 of the Salmon and Freshwater Fisheries (Consolidation) (Scotland) Act 2003 prohibits all modes of fishing for and taking salmon in a salmon fishery district during the annual close time. The exception to this is where provision has been made for periods within the annual close time during which fishing for and taking salmon by rod and line is permitted.

items.title: The Dumfries and Galloway Council (Garlieston) Harbour Empowerment Order 2008
items.description: This Order establishes Dumfries and Galloway Council as the harbour authority for the harbour of Garlieston. The Order confers duties and powers on the Council as harbour authority, and makes related provisions.

items.title: The Planning etc. (Scotland) Act 2006 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings section 55(2) of the Planning etc. (Scotland) Act 2006 (“the Act”) into force on 1st June 2008.

items.title: The Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2008
items.description: The Criminal Proceedings etc. (Reform) (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”) received Royal Assent on 22nd February 2007.

items.title: The Nature Conservation (Scotland) Act 2004 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings section 22 (SSSI register) of the Nature Conservation (Scotland) Act 2004 (“the Act”) into force on 2nd June 2008 (section 22(3)) and 30th June 2008 (section 22(1) and (2)). Section 22 is the last provision of the Act to be brought into force.

items.title: The Licensing (Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order makes provision relating to the granting of provisional licences under section 26 of the Licensing (Scotland) Act 1976 (“the 1976 Act”). This provision will be transitional in nature as it will cease to apply on 1st September 2009 on the full commencement of the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 and the full repeal of the 1976 Act in terms of article 3 of the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2007 (S.S.I. 2007/472).

items.title: The Strategic Development Planning Authority Designation (No. 1) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order designates, for the purposes of section 4(1) of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997, East Dunbartonshire Council, East Renfrewshire Council, Glasgow City Council, Inverclyde Council, North Lanarkshire Council, Renfrewshire Council, South Lanarkshire Council and West Dunbartonshire Council as authorities which are jointly to prepare and keep under review a plan (to be known as a “strategic development plan”) for an area, known as a “strategic development plan area”.

items.title: The Strategic Development Planning Authority Designation (No. 2) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order designates, for the purposes of section 4(1) of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997, Aberdeen City Council and Aberdeenshire Council as authorities which are jointly to prepare and keep under review a plan (to be known as a “strategic development plan”) for an area, known as a “strategic development plan area”.

items.title: The Strategic Development Planning Authority Designation (No. 3) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order designates, for the purposes of section 4(1) of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997, Angus Council, Dundee City Council, Fife Council and Perth and Kinross Council as authorities which are jointly to prepare and keep under review a plan (to be known as a “strategic development plan”) for an area, known as a “strategic development plan area”.

items.title: The Strategic Development Planning Authority Designation (No. 4) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order designates, for the purposes of section 4(1) of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997, City of Edinburgh Council, East Lothian Council, Fife Council, Midlothian Council, Scottish Borders Council and West Lothian Council as authorities which are jointly to prepare and keep under review a plan (to be known as a “strategic development plan”) for an area, known as a “strategic development plan area”.

items.title: The Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007 (Access to Land on Application) Order 2008
items.description: This Order makes provision as regards authorising prospective applicants for orders under section 1 of the Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007 (which relate to the authorisation of transport systems and inland waterways), to enter land to undertake inspections and surveys for purposes connected with construction, operation or works to which such an order would relate. This Order prescribes the manner in which an application should be made, the procedure for the making and consideration of representations in relation to an application and the procedure for enforcing access, where subsequent to authorisation, access is prevented.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Traffic Regulation Conditions) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 3 of the Public Service Vehicles (Traffic Regulation Conditions) Regulations 1986 by prescribing an additional purpose for which traffic commissioners may, pursuant to section 7 of the Transport Act 1985, determine traffic regulation conditions. Those conditions apply to local bus services registered under section 6 of that Act.

items.title: The Police Grant (Variation) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order varies the Police Grant (Scotland) Order 2007 for the financial year 2007/08 to state the actual aggregate police grant and the amounts of police grant paid to each police authority and joint police board in respect of that financial year, as redetermined on the basis of audited accounts.

items.title: The Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007 (Access to Land by the Scottish Ministers) Order 2008
items.description: This Order makes provision as regards entry to land by the Scottish Ministers, where they are considering publishing under section 6 of the Transport and Works (Scotland) Act 2007 a notice of a proposal to make an order under section 1 of that Act, to inspect and survey land for purposes connected with construction, operation or works to which such an order would relate. An order under section 1 of that Act relates to the authorisation of transport systems and inland waterways. This Order prescribes the procedure for the making and consideration of representations in relation to a proposal for entry and the procedure for enforcing access where, subsequent to a determination to take entry, access is prevented.

items.title: The Feed (Hygiene and Enforcement) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Feed (Hygiene and Enforcement) (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the Feed Hygiene Regulations”) and the Feeding Stuffs (Application to Zootechnical Additives etc.) (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the Zootechnical Additives Regulations”).

items.title: The National Scenic Areas (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend:

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) (Scotland) Order 1992 (“the 1992 Order”).

items.title: The Individual Learning Account (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Individual Learning Account (Scotland) Regulations 2004 (“the principal regulations”). The principal regulations specify arrangements which qualify under section 2 of the Education and Training (Scotland) Act 2000 and provide for the payment of grants in respect of persons who are parties to the qualifying arrangements.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Student Loans) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 to provide that part-time courses may no longer be designated courses. Students undertaking part-time courses will therefore no longer be eligible for student loans.

items.title: The Education (Means Testing) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Students' Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 2007, the Nursing and Midwifery Student Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 2007 and the Education (Student Loans) (Scotland) Regulations 2007.

items.title: The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.5) Order 2008
The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.5) Order 2008
The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.5) Order 2008
The Management of Offenders etc. (Scotland) Act 2005 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2008
items.description: This Order (Article 2) brings into force certain provisions in section 15 of the Management of Offenders etc. (Scotland) Act 2005 (asp 14) (“the 2005 Act”).

items.title: The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.5) Order 2008
The A76 Trunk Road (Newbridge Drive, Dumfries to Newbridge Village) (Redetermination of Means of Exercise of Public Right of Passage) Order 2008
The Academic Awards and Distinctions (UHI Millennium Institute) (Scotland) Order of Council 2008
items.description: This Order gives the UHI Millennium Institute the power to grant degrees, diplomas, certificates and other academic awards or distinctions except where the award or distinction, of whatever description, is made on completion of a programme of supervised research.

items.title: The Education (Assisted Places) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Assisted Places) (Scotland) Regulations 2001 to uprate the qualifying income levels for the remission of fees and charges and the making of grants under the assisted places scheme.

items.title: The St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the St Mary’s Music School (Aided Places) (Scotland) Regulations 2001 to uprate, with effect from 1st August 2008, the qualifying income levels for the remission of fees and charges and the making of grants under the aided places scheme.

items.title: The Feeding Stuffs (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations make further amendments to the Feeding Stuffs (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the Feeding Stuffs Regulations”).

items.title: The Spreadable Fats, Milk and Milk Products (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, provide for the execution and enforcement of certain provisions of Council Regulation (EC) No. 1234/2007 establishing a common organisation of agricultural markets and on specific provisions for certain agricultural products (Single CMO Regulation) (“the Council Regulation”).

items.title: The Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Conservation Bodies) Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Conservation Bodies) Order 2003 by adding Dundee Historic Environment Trust, Inverness City Heritage Trust and Sir Henry Wade’s Pilmuir Trust to the list of prescribed conservation bodies. Conservation bodies are bodies in favour of whom it is competent to create a conservation burden under section 38(1) of the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003.

items.title: The Official Feed and Food Controls (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Official Feed and Food Control (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the 2007 Regulations”) to add the Guar Gum (Restriction on First Placing on the Market) (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (“the 2008 Regulations”) to the definition of “relevant feed law” in Schedule 2 to the 2007 Regulations.

items.title: The Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order revokes and remakes the Diseases of Animals (Approved Disinfectants) Order 1978 in so far as it relates to Scotland.

items.title: The Abolition of Bridge Tolls (Scotland) Act 2008 (Commencement) Order 2008
items.description: This Order appoints 11th February 2008 as the day on which the Abolition of Bridge Tolls (Scotland) Act 2008, except section 4, will come into force. Section 4 relates to commencement and short title and came into force on Royal Assent, which was granted on 24th January 2008.

items.title: The Academic Awards and Distinctions (Additional Powers of the University of Aberdeen) Order of Council 2008
items.description: The power of the University of Aberdeen to confer degrees derives from the power vested in its Chancellor in persona by the University’s Papal Bull of 1495. This Order gives the University of Aberdeen the power to grant degrees and other academic awards and distinctions jointly with other institutions; to authorise other institutions to grant such awards on its behalf; and after due investigation, to deprive persons of such awards.

items.title: The Register of Sites of Special Scientific Interest (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of the form and content of the SSSI register (regulation 3). That register is constituted by section 22(1) of the Nature Conservation (Scotland) Act 2004 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Registration of Fish Farming and Shellfish Farming Businesses Amendment (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Registration of Fish Farming and Shellfish Farming Businesses Order 1985 (“the principal Order”). It substitutes for the forms set out in Schedules 4 and 5 to the principal Order revised forms to be used for the notification of escapes from fish farms.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Sheriff Court Rules) (Miscellaneous Amendments) 2008
items.description: (This note is not part of the Act of Sederunt)

items.title: The National Health Service Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations establish new pension arrangements for people employed in the National Health Service in Scotland on or after 1st April 2008 wishing to join the National Health Service Superannuation Scheme for Scotland (“the scheme”) or in such employment immediately before that date, but not already members of that scheme under the pension arrangements contained in the National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 1995 (S.I. 1995/365).

items.title: The National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Additional Voluntary Contributions, Injury Benefits and Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations further amend the National Health Superannuation Scheme (Scotland)(Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations 1998 (“the AVC Regulations”), the National Health Service (Scotland) (Injury Benefits) Regulations 1998 (“the Injury Benefits Regulations”) and the National Health Service (Compensation for Premature Retirement) (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (“the Compensation Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: This Instrument further amends the National Health Service Pension Scheme Regulations 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”).

items.title: The Teachers' Superannuation (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations make further amendments to the Teachers' Superannuation (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”), the Teachers' Superannuation (Additional Voluntary Contributions) (Scotland) Regulations 1995 (“the 1995 Regulations”) and the Teachers (Compensation for Premature Retirement and Redundancy) (Scotland) Regulations 1996 (“the 1996 Regulations”).

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Administration) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations set out provisions relating to the administration of the new Local Government Pension Scheme (“the Scheme”) which is to come into existence on 1st April 2009 and is constituted by these Regulations, the Local Government Pension Scheme (Benefits, Membership and Contributions) (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (“the Benefit Regulations”) and the Local Government Pension Scheme (Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2008.

items.title: The Local Government Pension Scheme (Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations set out transitional provisions relating to members of the Local Government Pension Scheme 1998 (“the 1998 Scheme”), which is to be replaced by the new Local Government Pension Scheme (Scotland) (“the Scheme”) with effect from 1st April 2009.

items.title: The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of  Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2008
The Local Government Pension Scheme (Benefits, Membership and Contributions) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations set out provisions relating to benefits, membership and contributions in the new Local Government Pension (Scotland) Scheme (“the Scheme”) which is to come into existence on 1st April 2009 and replace the Local Government Pension (Scotland) Scheme 1998 (“the 1998 Scheme”).

items.title: The A77 Trunk Road (Park End to Bennane Improvement) (Side Roads) Order 2008
The Offenders Assisting Investigations and Prosecutions (Substituted Sentences) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order makes provision in relation to the calculation of sentences following the imposition of a substitute sentence under section 92(7) or (9) or on appeal under section 92(11) of the Police, Public Order and Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2006 (“the 2006 Act”).

items.title: The Rural Development Contracts (Rural Priorities) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Rural Development Contracts (Rural Priorities) (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (“the 2008 Regulations”) which introduce measures to supplement Council Regulation (EC) No. 1698/2005 (O.J. No. L 277, 21.10.2005, p.1) (“the Council Regulation”) which lays down general rules governing Community support for rural development (financed by the European Agricultural Fund for Rural Development established by Council Regulation (EC) No. 1290/2005 (O.J. No. L 209, 11.8.2005, p.1)) and Commission Regulation (EC) Nos. 1974/2006 (O.J. No. L 368, 23.12.2006, p.15) and 1975/2006 (O.J. No. L 368, 23.12.2006, p.74) laying down detailed rules for the application of the Council Regulation.

items.title: The Bluetongue (Scotland) Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Bluetongue (Scotland) Order 2008 (“the principal Order”), mainly to make specific provision for vaccination of animals against bluetongue.

items.title: The Graduate Endowment (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations make provision for graduates liable to pay their graduate endowment on 1st April 2005, 2006 and 2007, but who have undertaken a further course or courses of higher education, to apply for a student loan to pay their graduate endowment on 1st April following the termination of their last course of higher education.

items.title: The Court of Session etc. Fees Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Court of Session etc. Fees Order 1997, which was last amended on 16th July 2007 by the Court of Session etc. Fees Amendment Order 2007 (S.S.I. 2007/319).

items.title: The High Court of Justiciary Fees Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order specifies new fee levels payable to the Principal Clerk of Justiciary or any officer acting for the Principal Clerk in relation to proceedings in the High Court of Justiciary. Fee levels were last amended on 16th July 2007 by the High Court of Justiciary Fees Amendment Order 2007 (S.S.I. 2007/321). These new fees will be applicable from 1st August 2008 and are found in the Table of Fees in Schedule 1 to this Order.

items.title: The Adults with Incapacity (Public Guardian’s Fees) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Adults with Incapacity (Public Guardian’s Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (“the 2008 Regulations”). New fee levels payable to the Public Guardian from 1st August 2008 are specified and reflect on average an increase of 8%. Fee levels were prescribed by the 2008 Regulations on 1st April 2008. The new fees are found in the Table of Fees in Schedule 1 to these Regulations.

items.title: The Sheriff Court Fees Amendment Order 2008
items.description: This Order amends the Sheriff Court Fees Order 1997.

items.title: The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2008
The Criminal Legal Assistance (Fees and Information etc.) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations relate to the different types of criminal legal assistance given by nominated and duty solicitors (where payments for the assistance come from the Scottish Legal Aid Fund).

items.title: The Fundable Bodies (The Scottish Agricultural College) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: The Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the Act”) established the Scottish Further and Higher Education Funding Council and made provision relating to the funding of bodies which provide further and higher education. Those bodies are described in the Act as “fundable bodies” and are listed in schedule 2 to the Act.

items.title: The A90 Trunk Road (Glendoick) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The Roads (Scotland) Act 1984 (Fixed Penalty) Regulations 2008
items.description: Section 37 of, and schedules 6 and 7 to, the Transport (Scotland) Act 2005 insert section 130A and Schedules 8A and 8B into the Roads (Scotland) Act 1984 (“the 1984 Act”). These provide for certain offences under the 1984 Act to become fixed penalty offences and enable regulations to be made in relation to them.

items.title: The Road Works (Fixed Penalty) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: Section 34 of, and schedules 4 and 5 to, the Transport (Scotland) Act 2005 insert section 154A and Schedules 6A and 6B into the New Roads and Street Works Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”). These provide for certain offences under Part 4 of the 1991 Act to become fixed penalty offences and enable regulations to be made in relation to them.

items.title: The Glasgow Commonwealth Games Act 2008 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2008
items.description: This Order appoints 20th June 2008 as the day on which sections 41 and 42 of the Glasgow Commonwealth Games Act 2008 (the “2008 Act”) will come into force. Sections 43 and 47 to 51 of the 2008 Act came into force on Royal Assent, which was granted on 10th June 2008.

items.title: The Electricity Works (Environmental Impact Assessment) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Electricity Works (Environmental Impact Assessment) (Scotland) Regulations 2000 (S.I. 2000/320) (“the principal Regulations”), which extend to Scotland only.

items.title: The Mobility and Access Committee for Scotland Revocation Regulations 2008 Revocation Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations revoke the Mobility and Access Committee for Scotland Revocation Regulations 2008.

items.title: The Public Transport Users' Committee for Scotland Amendment Order 2008 Revocation Order 2008
items.description: This Order revokes the Public Transport Users' Committee for Scotland Amendment Order 2008.

items.title: The A737/A738 Trunk Road (Park Lane, Old Woodwynd Road, The Meadows, Claremont Crescent, Kilwinning) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2008
The A977 Trunk Road (Kincardine) (30mph and 40mph Speed Limit) Order 2008
The Advice and Assistance (Limits, Conditions and Representation) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations make changes to existing regulations which respectively concern financial limits, financial conditions and assistance by way of representation.

items.title: The A9 Trunk Road (Aberuthven to Loaninghead) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The Public Service Vehicles (Registration of Local Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations increase the fees for the registration of, or the variation of the registration of, a local bus service, other than a community bus service. The fee is increased from £54 to £57 (an increase of 5.6%). The fees were last increased on 1st April 2007.

items.title: The M77/A77 Trunk Road (Bogend Toll) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.6) Order 2008
The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.6) Order 2008
The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.6) Order 2008
The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.6) Order 2008
The Human Tissue (Scotland) Act 2006 (Consequential Amendment) Order 2008
items.description: This Order makes an amendment to the Asylum and Immigration (Treatment of Claimants, etc.) Act 2004 (“the 2004 Act”), which is considered to be necessary in consequence of the Human Tissue (Scotland) Act 2006 (“the 2006 Act”). The 2006 Act repealed the Human Organ Transplants Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”) in so far as it has effect in Scotland to make replacement provision for Scotland with regard to transplantation. As a consequence, the reference in section 4(4)(b) of the 2004 Act to offences committed under the 1989 Act is replaced with a reference to the 2006 Act.

items.title: The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) Order 2008
The Protection of Children (Scotland) Act 2003 (Amendment of the Definition of Child Care Position) Order 2008
items.description: This Order is made under the Protection of Children (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”) which provides for a list of individuals considered unsuitable to work with children to be established and maintained by the Scottish Ministers and for those on the list to be prohibited from working with children.

items.title: The Plastic Materials and Articles in Contact with Food (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations provide for enforcement of Commission Regulation (EC) No. 597/2008 amending Commission Regulation (EC) No. 372/2007 laying down transitional migration limits for plasticisers in gaskets in lids intended to come into contact with foods (O.J. No. L 164, 25.06.2008, p.12) (“the Commission Regulation”).

items.title: The Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 1992 Modification Order 2008
items.description: This Order modifies the Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 1992 by inserting new subsections (1ZA) and (11) into section 47. Section 47(1) gives powers to the Secretary of State, and now to the Scottish Ministers, to close a designated institution and wind up its governing body. The modifications require the Scottish Ministers, in the case of designated institutions for which section 47 forms part of the constitution within the meaning of the Charities and Trustee Investment (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the 2005 Act”), to obtain the consent of the governing body prior to exercising those powers.

items.title: The Water Environment (Relevant Enactments and Designation of Responsible Authorities and Functions) Order 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order specifies the enactments set out in Schedule 1 as relevant enactments within the meaning of section 2 of the Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 and revokes the previous Order specifying relevant enactments.

items.title: The Crime (International Co-operation) Act 2003 (Designation of Participating Countries) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: This Order designates the United States of America as a participating country for the purposes of sections 37, 40, 43, 44 and 45 of the Crime (International Co-operation) Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”).

items.title: The Nutritional Requirements for Food and Drink in Schools (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations prescribe nutritional requirements for food and drink in schools. They apply to public schools and grant aided schools as well as hostels which are provided and maintained by education authorities under the Education (Scotland) Act 1980.

items.title: The Control of Salmonella in Poultry (Scotland) Order 2008 (revoked)
items.description: This Order revokes and remakes the Poultry Breeding Flocks and Hatcheries (Scotland) Order 2007. The principal change is that it extends the sampling provision in the earlier Order to laying flocks (the earlier Order was confined to breeding flocks) of birds of the species Gallus gallus.

items.title: The M90 Trunk Road (Gairneybridge to Milnathort) (Temporary 50mph Speed Limit) Order 2008
The Charity Test (Specified Bodies) (Scotland) Order 2008
items.description: By this Order Scottish Ministers have disapplied paragraph (b) of section 7(4) of the Charities and Trustee Investment (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the 2005 Act”) in relation to the bodies specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 8) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings into force further provisions of the Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”). The provisions come into force on 10th July 2008 and provide as follows:–

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges for Drugs and Appliances) (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations provide for the making and recovery of charges for drugs and appliances (other than dental or optical appliances) supplied under or by virtue of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 (“the Act”).

items.title: The A737/A738 Trunk Road (Howgate, Kilwinning to Cockenzie Road, Dalvargen) (Temporary Width and Weight Restriction of Traffic) Order 2008
The A82 Trunk Road (Scottish Open Golf Tournament, Loch Lomond) (Special Event) (Temporary Restriction of Speed) Order 2008
The A92/A972 Trunk Road (Scott Fyffe Roundabout, Dundee to Pitkerro Roundabout, Dundee) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The M90/A90 Trunk Road (Bruntland Road Junction, Portlethen) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The A96 Trunk Road (Moss Street, Keith) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules Amendment No. 3) (Seizure and Disposal of Vehicles) 2008
items.description: (This note is not part of the Act of Adjournal)

items.title: The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.7) Order 2008
The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.7) Order 2008
The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.7) Order 2008
The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.7) Order 2008
The Housing (Scotland) Act 2001 (Alteration of Housing Finance Arrangements) Order 2008
items.description: Part V of the Housing (Scotland) Act 2001 (“the 2001 Act”) provides for a new funding regime for the strategic housing functions of local authorities. Section 94 of the 2001 Act allows Scottish Ministers by order from a specified date to remove the application of sections 191 to 193 and 203(1) of the Housing (Scotland) Act 1987 (“the 1987 Act”) in relation to specified local authorities. Sections 191 to 193 of the 1987 Act impose a duty on Scottish Ministers to provide housing revenue grant to local authorities and section 203(1) of that Act places a corresponding duty on local authorities to maintain a housing revenue account.

items.title: The A84/A85 Trunk Road (Strathyre) (30mph and 40mph Speed Limits) Order 2008
The A90 Trunk Road (B957 Tannadice Junction) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2008
The Adoption and Children (Scotland) Act 2007 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st September 2008 sections 62 to 65 of the Adoption and Children (Scotland) Act 2007.

items.title: The Housing Grants (Application Forms) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the application forms for improvement grant not relating to works for a disabled occupant, for improvement grant relating to works for a disabled occupant, for repairs grant and for grant for a means of escape from fire in a house in multiple occupation in the Housing Grants (Application Forms) (Scotland) Regulations 2003.

items.title: The South West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.8) Order 2008
The South East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.8) Order 2008
The North West Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.8) Order 2008
The North East Unit Trunk Roads Area (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic, Temporary Prohibitions of Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.8) Order 2008
The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2003 (“the 2003 Regulations”).

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Regulations 1998 (“the 1998 Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made by means of a voucher system in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Sea Fish (Prohibited Methods of Fishing) (Firth of Clyde) Order 2008
items.description: This Order prohibits, subject to certain exceptions, all methods of fishing within certain areas of the Firth of Clyde from 14th February to 30th April 2008. The Order is made pursuant to Article 46 of Council Regulation (EC) No. 850/98 of 30th March 1998 for the conservation of fishery resources through technical measures for the protection of juveniles of marine organisms. A map showing the areas closed to fishing under this Order is appended to this note for illustrative purposes only. The Order remains in force until the end of 30th April 2008 (article 1(2)).

items.title: The National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Charges to Overseas Visitors) (Scotland) Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”), which provide for the making and recovery of charges in respect of certain services provided under the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978 to certain persons not ordinarily resident in the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Public Contracts and Utilities Contracts (Common Procurement Vocabulary Codes) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the Public Contracts Regulations”) and the Utilities Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the Utilities Contracts Regulations”).

items.title: The Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 (Commencement No. 5) Order 2008
items.description: This Order brings section 123 (excluded premises) of the Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 into force on 5th September 2008.

items.title: The Divorce etc. (Pensions) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations amend the Divorce etc. (Pensions) (Scotland) Regulations 2000 in consequence of the UK government’s resumption of direct responsibility for determining how cash equivalent transfer values (“cash equivalents”) of pension rights are to be calculated. From 1st October 2008, the Actuarial Profession’s GN11 guidance no longer applies and cash equivalents are to be calculated in accordance with rules set out in the Occupational Pension Schemes (Transfer Values) Regulations 1996, as amended by the Occupational Pension Schemes (Transfer Values) (Amendment) Regulations 2008. These Regulations provide for the same methodology to be used for valuation of pensions in connection with divorce or dissolution of civil partnerships.

items.title: The Less Favoured Area Support Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2008 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Less Favoured Area Support Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Authorised Fuels) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to Scotland only, declare the fuels which are authorised fuels for the purposes of Part III of the Clean Air Act 1993. The authorised fuels are described in regulation 2 and the Schedule.

items.title: The Smoke Control Areas (Exempt Fireplaces) (Scotland) (No. 2) Order 2008
items.description: This Order, which extends to Scotland only, makes provision for exemption from the prohibition, under section 20 of the Clean Air Act 1993, of the emission of smoke in smoke control areas.

items.title: The Freedom of Information (Scotland) Act 2002 (Scottish Public Authorities) Amendment Order 2008
items.description: The obligations under the Freedom of Information (Scotland) Act 2002 apply to Scottish public authorities. For the purposes of the Act, a “Scottish public authority” means a body or office which is listed in schedule 1 to the Act or designated by Order under section 5, or a publicly owned company as defined by section 6. The list in schedule 1 to the Act may be amended, by adding or removing references to bodies or offices, by Order under section 4(1) of the Act.

items.title: The Action Programme for Nitrate Vulnerable Zones (Scotland) Regulations 2008
items.description: These Regulations establish a revised action programme for the nitrate vulnerable zones which were designated in Scotland by regulation 3 of the Designation of Nitrate Vulnerable Zones (Scotland) Regulations 2002 and regulation 3 of the Designation of Nitrate Vulnerable Zones (No. 2) (Scotland) Regulations 2002.
